title: Beginner's guide
text:
# Beginner's guide

Source: https://cs2.paradoxwikis.com/Beginner%27s_guide

This article istimelessand should be accurate for any version of the game.

This article or section needs verification.

- The information in this article or section may not be reliable.
- Reason: Most of this article was copied from the Cities Skylines 1 wiki and not all of it might apply to Cities Skylines II
- If you have knowledge of this topic please verify the information, otherwise discuss it in thetalk page.

This page is intended as a list of tips that might not be entirely obvious to newer players ofCities: Skylines II, but which once explained can make the game more enjoyable.

## Road-building basics Main article:Roads

title: Beginner's guide
text:
nded as a list of tips that might not be entirely obvious to newer players ofCities: Skylines II, but which once explained can make the game more enjoyable.

## Road-building basics Main article:Roads

- Adjusting the road's elevation for bridges and tunnels with thePage UpandPage Downkeys. (D-Pad up and down for console players).
- Add roundabouts to busy intersections to reduce traffic.
- Grid layouts are king. Blocks of road rectangles can help services and people move around extremely quickly. Build a grid-like road structure in busy city centers.
- Avoid the problem of "stroads": they try to be both a high-speed road and a slow speed street simultaneously. They feature multiple wide lanes like highways, but are interrupted every block by traffic lights, driveways, and turning traffic like city streets. This hybrid approach fails spectacularly at both functions, creating dangerous, congested, and economically unproductive corridors.

## Basic services and early budget management Main article:Services
Main article:Taxation

title: Beginner's guide
section: Basic services and early budget management Main article:Services
text:
ails spectacularly at both functions, creating dangerous, congested, and economically unproductive corridors.

## Basic services and early budget management Main article:Services
Main article:Taxation

- Pause the simulation when you start to build the necessary infrastructure, or your upkeep costs will drain funds before citizens move in. However, citizens aren't moving in or paying taxes when the game is paused, so you can quickly run out of money by building too much.
- You get a money bonus when you reach milestones.
- After unlocking taxes, increasing the tax on higher education levels won't lead to high taxes complaints because of the salary well-educated citizens make.
- Always have your water pumping stationupstreamof your sewage drain pipe and a comfortable distance away, or you'll pollute the drinking water and poison your citizens. If your bodies of water don't have strong currents, use groundwater pumping stations on non-polluted groundwater.

## Transportation Main article:Transportation

Public transportation reduces traffic by offering your citizens an alternative to driving, and is essential for creating an effective city.

title: Beginner's guide
text:
groundwater.

## Transportation Main article:Transportation

Public transportation reduces traffic by offering your citizens an alternative to driving, and is essential for creating an effective city.

- Start using public transportation as soon as possible, and try to keep the lines simple.
- Buses are inexpensive and effective, but too many buses overlapping can cause congestion.
- Metro (subway) is the most effective transportation system, but it's also costly. Buses that complement your metro system is the most effective solution.
- Passenger trains aren't useful for transport within a city. They become effective if you want to bringtouriststo your city, especially in large, sprawling cities that are widely dispersed.
- Use dedicated passenger railways to prevent rail line congestion.
- Longer lines with more stops will spawn more vehicles.
- Public transportation stops are a type of service and can be used to increase happiness and building level.
- Public transportation lines are most effective when their routes pass through all types of zoning (except office since they're not much of a traffic burden).
- Commuter traffic is most common in commercial areas and industrial traffic is more common between industries and occasionally, between industrial and commercial areas.
- Public transportation is in highest demand within commercial areas.
- Use cargo rail in industrial districts—they will greatly help develop the district and remove part of the traffic. Remember that a good road system is needed to serve the cargo station, as every time a train arrives, a lot of trucks are moving the goods to their destination. (One-way streets are one of the solutions here)

title: Beginner's guide
text:
that a good road system is needed to serve the cargo station, as every time a train arrives, a lot of trucks are moving the goods to their destination. (One-way streets are one of the solutions here)

## Services Main article:Services

- Note that services with vehicles can travel over the entire map (provided there are road connections), but there is a coverage range for the purposes of leveling up buildings and managing well-being. The associated infoviews will show you which parts of the city are within range. When placing a service building, green roads indicate the extent of that coverage. Insufficient coverage will result in buildings not leveling up, reduced well-being, and therefore less tax income.
- Certain service buildings, such as landfills and cemeteries, must be completely empty to relocate or be bulldozed. Garbage or dead bodies can be reallocated to other facilities by using its service vehicles for the tasking instead of normal collection duties.
- Parks and services increase land value, increasing popularity of given land, as well as stimulating those areas to develop.

## Planning

title: Beginner's guide
text:
vehicles for the tasking instead of normal collection duties.
- Parks and services increase land value, increasing popularity of given land, as well as stimulating those areas to develop.

## Planning

- Try to split your truck traffic away from your commutertraffic. Having both loads of traffic on the same roads can quickly snarl even well-designed traffic systems. Creating a district with the Heavy Bancan help accomplish this.
- Creating a highway that will be the "spine" of your city can be beneficial for the traffic flow; however, a proper road hierarchy is needed in order to maintain the flow in the city and on the highway.
- Strategic roundabouts shouldn't be small (the smaller the roundabout, the more the cars have to slow down, thus causing a decrease in the flow of traffic).
- Try to separatepollution-heavy buildings/industries from residential areas.
- Farming and forestry specialized industryproduce no pollution, and their natural resource can be artificially created and renewable.
- Pedestrian pathways: Just because they are under the "landscaping" section does not mean you should write them off as just decorative fluff. They are legitimately useful in cutting down your traffic. Aresidentwalking to work is one less car clogging your road. It's like free mass transit. You can also make pedestrian paths elevated the way you can elevate roads. They permit steeper inclines than even the smallest roads.
- with decorative trees or sound barriers can reduce noise pollution.
- Officescan be used as a barrier between commercial and residential areas to reduce noise in residential areas.
- Zoning plays an important role in traffic. To isolate commuter and industrial traffic from each other, zone commercial area at the middle, residential and industrial on either side of commercial area. Offices can be zoned anywhere since they simply provide jobs only and are less burden on transportation system.
- Railwayscan get congested, just like roadways. To help prevent railway congestion, it may be beneficial to segregate freight and passenger lines. Additionally, bypasses can be made to go around stations. When creating rail intersections, it's best to join them in such a way that a full train can fit on the interchange, which will allow following trains traveling to a different destination to continue unimpeded.
- Great ideas for intersections can be found here: <http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Interchange_(road)#System_interchange>. If you still have trouble, you can get out a piece of paper and draw some intersections you think are best. If you live near a highway or city, take a look at the interstate intersections. Most likely those will help out.

title: Beginner's guide
text:
uble, you can get out a piece of paper and draw some intersections you think are best. If you live near a highway or city, take a look at the interstate intersections. Most likely those will help out.

# Principles

Apply all eight principles simultaneously for maximum effect. Your next city will be perfect.

## Value Zones

title: Beginner's guide
text:
ke a look at the interstate intersections. Most likely those will help out.

# Principles

Apply all eight principles simultaneously for maximum effect. Your next city will be perfect.

## Value Zones

- Place high density commercial and residential in your city center where transit converges. Medium density fills the middle ring where arterial roads meet local streets. Single family homes belong on the periphery near industrial zones. This is both realistic and financially optimal.
- High value properties generate more tax revenue per square meter of infrastructure. Your sewage pipes maintenance cost the same whether they serve a $50,000 single family home or a $500,000 one-hundred unit apartment building.
- Suburbs/Strode environments generate remarkably little tax revenue per acre compared to traditional high-density urban development. The landscape of parking lots, single-story buildings, and wide setbacks produces a fraction of the property tax revenue needed to support the infrastructure serving it.
- Most players build uniform zones across their entire map, then wonder why their budget crashes. The property tax from one downtown high rise equals ten suburban houses, but requires one tenth the road maintenance.
- Zone your most expensive commercial towers where subway lines intersect. Build luxury apartments within walking distance of premium office space. Reserve cheap suburban sprawl for city edges where land value naturally decreases.

title: Beginner's guide
text:
mercial towers where subway lines intersect. Build luxury apartments within walking distance of premium office space. Reserve cheap suburban sprawl for city edges where land value naturally decreases.

Real World Examples:

- Downtown Tokyo land costs $10,000 per square foot while suburbs run $50. Your city should mirror this gradient.
- Amsterdam's canal ring demonstrates this perfectly. The city center contains five story mixed use buildings worth millions, while outer neighborhoods feature two story rowhouses.

## Transit First

title: Beginner's guide
text:
.
- Amsterdam's canal ring demonstrates this perfectly. The city center contains five story mixed use buildings worth millions, while outer neighborhoods feature two story rowhouses.

## Transit First

- Your metro system should precede zoning decisions. Place stations at major intersections first, then zone high-density within a five-minute walk. Medium density extends another ten minutes out. Low density fills the gaps. This creates natural transit ridership that pays operational costs.
- Stations anchor neighborhoods with 50-story towers surrounding each stop. Your virtual citizens behave identically-they'll ride transit when destinations cluster near stations.
- Most players zone first, then add transit as an afterthought. That backwards approach creates car dependency and never-ending traffic gridlock.
- One metro line serving clustered high rises moves more people in per hour than six bus routes through sprawling suburbs in a day.
- Mark future metro stations on your map first. Draw lines connecting major employment centers. Only after transit placement should you begin zoning.
- Dense residential goes near stations. Commercial fills the gaps. Office towers cluster at transfer points. This creates walkable neighborhoods where citizens abandon cars and traffic is almost non-existent.

Real World Examples:

title: Beginner's guide
text:
stations. Commercial fills the gaps. Office towers cluster at transfer points. This creates walkable neighborhoods where citizens abandon cars and traffic is almost non-existent.

Real World Examples:

- New York built elevated rail and subway lines before skyscrapers appeared; this supercharged its demand for increasingly taller skyscrapers.
- Singapore's MRT system demonstrates this principle.

## Mixed Use

- Your neighborhoods needs diversity. Combine residential, commercial, and office zones within walking distance. Workers live above shops. Restaurants serve nearby apartment residents. This reduces traffic because people walk instead of drive. Residents complete daily errands without vehicles.
- Your city needs corner stores, not car-dependent strip malls that takes an hour to drive to. North American-style segregation "all homes here, all shops there" maximizes driving. Mixed neighborhoods minimize it.
- Place commercial strips through residential areas. Add office clusters near apartments.
- Most players segregate land uses because zoning tools encourage it. Fight this tendency. Real cities thrive on diversity. Your traffic problems disappear when people walk to destinations.
- Zone residential blocks, then paint commercial along every third street. Add office space near transit hubs.

Real World Examples:

title: Beginner's guide
text:
. Your traffic problems disappear when people walk to destinations.
- Zone residential blocks, then paint commercial along every third street. Add office space near transit hubs.

Real World Examples:

- Paris zoning laws mixes groceries, bakeries, apartments, and offices on every block.
- Barcelona's superblocks prove this works; each nine block area contains homes, shops, and workplaces; all within a five-minute walk from each other.
- Copenhagen is a perfect model, with shops on ground floors and apartments or offices above.

## Road Tiers

title: Beginner's guide
text:
area contains homes, shops, and workplaces; all within a five-minute walk from each other.
- Copenhagen is a perfect model, with shops on ground floors and apartments or offices above.

## Road Tiers

- Highways connect regions without intersections. Arterials move traffic between districts with limited access. Collectors gather traffic from neighborhoods. Local streets serve individual buildings.
- Most players build six lane roads everywhere, creating dangerousstroadsthat fail at safety, speed and access. A proper hierarchy separates through traffic from local access. Residential streets shouldnever connectdirectly to highways.
- Force traffic through collectors and arterials. This prevents cut-through traffic ruining neighborhoods while maintaining citywide connectivity.
- Intersections are the Achilles heel of roads: the weak link in the system. They cause delays for drivers and despite being a sliver of the entire road network, account for half of injury crashes. Instead only use roundabouts, T-intersections, diverging diamonds lanes, continuous flow intersections and U-turns. Avoid forcing vehicles into making left-turns at all costs!

Real World Examples:

title: Beginner's guide
text:
s. Instead only use roundabouts, T-intersections, diverging diamonds lanes, continuous flow intersections and U-turns. Avoid forcing vehicles into making left-turns at all costs!

Real World Examples:

- Los Angeles has twelve lane highways feeding two-lane residential streets; your road network needs this hierarchy.
- London's road system follows these principles; the M25 motorway circles the city; A-roads connect districts; B-roads serve neighborhoods; Residential streets access homes.
- Chicago's grid system includes this hierarchy; Lake Shore Drive handles regional traffic; Division Street moves traffic between neighborhoods; Side streets access individual buildings.

## Green Links

title: Beginner's guide
text:
Chicago's grid system includes this hierarchy; Lake Shore Drive handles regional traffic; Division Street moves traffic between neighborhoods; Side streets access individual buildings.

## Green Links

- Your green spaces should form continuous networks. Link neighborhood parks through planted streets. Connect district parks via greenways. Join regional parks with protected corridors. This creates an aesthetically pleasing ecological networks supporting urban wildlife while providing walking routes between destinations.
- Parks connect through green streets forming a continuous system. Your citizens walk these pleasant routes instead of concrete sidewalks.
- Trees reduce heat, manage stormwater, and increase property values significantly.
- Most players scatter parks randomly in inaccessible regions across their city. This wastes their potential. Connected green space multiplies benefits.
- A continuous park system provides recreation, transportation, and environmental services. Plant street trees along every collector and arterial. Create park chains through neighborhoods.
- Your property values will increase fifteen percent near green corridors. Plangreen corridorsbefore dense development. Connect neighborhood parks through planted boulevards. Link district parks with dedicated paths. Citizens will walk these routes for pleasure, reducing traffic and car dependency while improving property values.

title: Beginner's guide
text:
ghborhood parks through planted boulevards. Link district parks with dedicated paths. Citizens will walk these routes for pleasure, reducing traffic and car dependency while improving property values.

Real World Examples:

- Copenhagen connects every park with tree-lined boulevards.
- Portland's Emerald Necklace demonstrates this concept.
- Boston's park system connects the Common to Franklin Park through continuous greenways.

## Buffer Zones

- Always separate incompatible land uses with transition zones. Commercial strips buffer residential from industrial areas. Green parks separate busy roads from quiet neighborhoods. Office parks transition between factories and homes. These buffers protect property values while maintaining city function. Your factory smoke shouldn't reach apartments.
- Most players zone residential directly against industrial areas, then complain about pollution. Commercial zones make perfect buffers; businesses don't mind noise or smell so long as the land lease is cheap.
- Place retail strips between factories and homes. Add office parks as secondary barriers. Your residential property values will double when separated from industrial pollution.
- also benefits because workers can walk from nearby commercial areas to factories. Each transition reduces incompatibility.

Real World Examples:

title: Beginner's guide
text:
will double when separated from industrial pollution.
- also benefits because workers can walk from nearby commercial areas to factories. Each transition reduces incompatibility.

Real World Examples:

- Houston places refineries and red-light districts beside elementary schools;Don't replicate this disaster!
- Tokyo uses this strategy perfectly; industrial zones border commercial areas, which transition to residential through office districts.
- Berlin demonstrates ideal buffering; Industrial areas border commercial zones; Commercial zones transition to offices; Offices buffer residential neighborhoods.

## Water Base

title: Beginner's guide
text:
ough office districts.
- Berlin demonstrates ideal buffering; Industrial areas border commercial zones; Commercial zones transition to offices; Offices buffer residential neighborhoods.

## Water Base

- Water infrastructure determines city growth. Plan utility corridors before placing zones. Main sewage lines follow valleys using gravity. Water pipes trace ridgelines maintaining pressure. Power lines parallel major roads and railways for maintenance access. This invisible infrastructure costs more than roads but gets ignored by most players.
- Most players zone everywhere, then discover sewage can't reach hilltops while floodwater pools in valleys. Plan infrastructure first.
- Mark sewage treatment locations based on natural water flow. Identify water source elevation. Zone only where utilities can reach. One sewage backup ruins twenty buildings and costs millions in repairs.
- Map elevation changes first. Plan sewage plants in valleys. Position water towers on high ground. Run power along major roads. Only after infrastructure placement should zoning begin.
- Most players paint zones randomly, then struggle with utility coverage. Build water networks first. Plan sewage systems carefully. Your development will proceed smoothly when infrastructure precedes zoning.

Real World Examples:

title: Beginner's guide
text:
domly, then struggle with utility coverage. Build water networks first. Plan sewage systems carefully. Your development will proceed smoothly when infrastructure precedes zoning.

Real World Examples:

- Ancient Rome built roads and aqueducts before buildings.
- Amsterdam's canal system shows proper planning; waterways provided sewage, transportation, and foundations.
- London's Victorian sewers still function because engineers planned for growth.

## Staged Growth

title: Beginner's guide
text:
dam's canal system shows proper planning; waterways provided sewage, transportation, and foundations.
- London's Victorian sewers still function because engineers planned for growth.

## Staged Growth

- DO NOT expand your city too quickly at the very beginning, because your budget will get drained earlier than expected; it is enough to start with a residential area, electricity, waterworks and sewer.
- Don't buy something until you need it—most things have an upkeep cost. If you can get by without it for a while, you will build more reserve money when you need it. The exception to this is the beginning of the game where you want to expand quickly to put your budget in the black (or green, as it were).
- Develop one neighborhood completely before starting another. Build core infrastructure first-transit, utilities, services. Zone residential and commercial in phases. Add parks and amenities as population grows. This prevents sprawling abandonment where services can't reach distant zones.
- Your budget benefits from concentrated growth. Services like police and fire have limited range. Most players paint zones across their entire map, then watch as distant areas lack services and collapse. Staged development concentrates municipal services. One fire station serves four complete neighborhoods but can't protect eight half-built districts. Start small, grow systematically, finish completely.
- Build one neighborhood to completion. Add all services and amenities. Only then begin adjacent development.

title: Beginner's guide
text:
t protect eight half-built districts. Start small, grow systematically, finish completely.
- Build one neighborhood to completion. Add all services and amenities. Only then begin adjacent development.

Real World Examples:

- Chicago grew in concentric rings over 150 years; your city needs similar patience.
- Stockholm's development followed this pattern; the city expanded systematically, completing each district before beginning the next.
- Vienna demonstrates perfect staged growth; The Ringstrasse developed before outer districts; Each expansion completed fully before beginning the next.

# Other

- Don'texpand your city too quickly at the very beginning, because your budget will get drained earlier than expected. You can start with a residential area, electricity, waterworks, and sewer.
- Save your city before you build a hydroelectric power plant, as sometimes the results might be different from your expectations. They also increase in cost with the length of the dam.
- Don't buy something until you need it—most things have an upkeep cost. If you can get by without it for awhile, you'll build more reserve money when you need it. The exception to this is the beginning of the game where you want to expand quickly to put your budget in the higher green.

Source: https://cs2.paradoxwikis.com/Bridges_and_Ports

Paradox/Steam

TheBridges and Portsexpansion pack was released on 2025-10-29, alongside theCold Wave Channelmusic pack[1] andPatch 1.3.6f1[2].

## Official description

Livelier coasts are now available with this new expansion,Bridges and Ports!

With a new set of tools you are now able to create a bustling port for your city, and add beautiful new details like drawbridges and lighthouses. With over 100 new assets as well as the new addition ofMarine Industriesyou can expand your waterfront landscape and connect your city in exciting new ways.

## Features

- Link your city to the sea with 20 new drawbridges and lift bridges.
- Ferries.
- Nine new parks, leisure piers in different sizes and quays that handle anything from simple paths to multi-lane roads.
- Four new lighthouses, one of which doubles as a tourist landmark.
- Customizable harbors.
- Marine Industries.

- Fishing Areas, and Fish farms.
 - Offshore Oil industry.

- Five newmaps, including a peninsula, a bay, and vast great lakes.
- Six newachievements.
- Over 100 new assets.

title: Citizens
text:
# Citizens

Source: https://cs2.paradoxwikis.com/Citizens

Please help with verifying or updating older sections of this article.
At least some were last verified forversion1.0.

Citizens act as one of the primary agents simulated in the game. They are the residents of the houses in the residential zones, they are the workforce of the industrial, commercial, and office zones, and they are the customers in the commercial zones. Citizens suffer if the services do not operate properly but they also reap the benefits and flourish if you manage to govern your city well and answer their various needs.

Citizens are always up to something choosing from a variety of different activities. These include mundane things such as going to work or school, shopping, sleeping, or studying. They also have rarer activities such as committing a crime, escaping the crime scene, seeking medical treatment, and moving away. Many of these actions have simulation effects such as traveling to different places causing traffic on the roads, shopping causing changes in the economy simulation (albeit small when considering individual households), and getting medical treatment which takes up patient spots at the medical facility.

title: Citizens
text:
the roads, shopping causing changes in the economy simulation (albeit small when considering individual households), and getting medical treatment which takes up patient spots at the medical facility.

Citizens can own pets. Pet owners are chosen randomly from the citizen pool, and while pets are purely cosmetic and do not affect citizen Happiness or their consumption of Resources, garbage accumulation, or noise pollution, they add a bit of flavor to your city.

## Age

With age, citizens in the game progress through four life stages:

1. Childrencannot work and receive a health boost. They start as Uneducated and will always go to Elementary School if there is capa city.
2. Teensreceive a health boost. They can work, but many will try to continue their education. When traveling by car or public transport they choose the cheapest route.
3. Adultscan work but some will prefer to continue their education. When traveling by car or public transport they choose the quickest route.
4. Seniors- cannot work and slowly lose maximum health until they eventually die. They cannot increase their education. When traveling by car or public transport they choose the most comfortable route.

## Happiness

title: Citizens
text:
not work and slowly lose maximum health until they eventually die. They cannot increase their education. When traveling by car or public transport they choose the most comfortable route.

## Happiness

Citizen Happiness is the overall value that represents a citizen’s satisfaction in their life. It is the combination of their Well-being and Health and affects the citizen’s behavior and their ability to work. It is always visible to the player in the citizen’s info panel and hovering over the family’s combined Happiness level with the mouse cursor in the household’s info panel shows detailed information about all the things that contribute to the value.

### Happiness factors

FactorTypePositive impactNegative impact

EducationservicesQualityProximity, easy access, and open capa city of education buildingsEducation buildings are difficult to access due to traffic, full capa city, etc.

LeisuretimeAbundance

Proximity & quick access to relaxation and enrichment such asparksandlandmarksFew if any parks, which may be crowded and far away.

EntertainmentAbundance

Easy access to Commerce, especially clubs and bars. Entertainment is difficult to access due to traffic, lack of transportation options, etc.

HealthcarecoverageReliabilityHealthcare buildings are nearby or quick to respond. Healthcare buildings are far away, overwhelmed, or take too long to arrive.

title: Citizens
text:
fic, lack of transportation options, etc.

HealthcarecoverageReliabilityHealthcare buildings are nearby or quick to respond. Healthcare buildings are far away, overwhelmed, or take too long to arrive.

MailserviceReliabilityNearby post office or mailbox, and fast postal sorting. No nearby post offices or mailboxes. Mail sorting demand exceeds capa city.

InternetserviceReliabilityHigh network capa city & good coverage. Low network capa city or coverage.

Noise pollutionAmountNoise pollution is low or limited. Noise pollution is prevalent or excessive. Noise pollution in residential areas is undesirable for citizens.

Citizen wealthAmountIncome and/or net worth are high. Income and/or net worth are low.

Size of homesAmountLarger homes (lower density)Smaller homes (higher density)

Tax levelsAmountLower taxesHigher taxes

### Well-being

title: Citizens
text:
come and/or net worth are high. Income and/or net worth are low.

Size of homesAmountLarger homes (lower density)Smaller homes (higher density)

Tax levelsAmountLower taxesHigher taxes

### Well-being

Well-being indicates the citizens’ mental state and sense of safety. Satisfying their basic needs such as having reliableelectricityand cleanwateras well as sufficientsewagetreatment so their toilets don’t overflow, will go a long way in building a solid base for their Well-being. Taking care of thegarbagepiling up and planningresidentialareas to avoid air, ground, and noisepollutionbuilds on that foundation, while good city service coverage provides a passive boost to citizen Well-being. Furthermore, you will want to ensure your citizens feel safe as living in areas with high crime probability decreases their Well-being.

Amenities such as a functioningpostalservice and high-speedinternetconnection are valued by citizens along with a suitable-sized apartment that matches their household’s needs. Families tend to look for larger homes in low density housing areas and small apartment buildings while singles and students can still flourish in compact apartments such as those available in tall residential towers and low rent apartment blocks. The citizens are consumers at heart and having options to buy and consumeGoodspositively affects their Well-being.

#### Leisure

title: Citizens
text:
ose available in tall residential towers and low rent apartment blocks. The citizens are consumers at heart and having options to buy and consumeGoodspositively affects their Well-being.

#### Leisure

Citizens love their free time and want to spend it in the city visitingparksand otherattractions, goingshopping, and enjoying entertainment in the various restaurants and bars. This need for Leisure affects their Well-being and when their Leisure decreases, they seek opportunities to increase it. Not all options are created equally though, which means it takes a different amount of time to gain Leisure in different places. Citizens choose the location based on the amount of and how quickly they can gain Leisure while staying there as well as comparing the pathfinding costs to the different locations.

Leisure can be gained from various locations. Cityrecreationincludes indoor and outdoor recreation possibilities in the form of tourist attractions andlandmarks, parks, plazas, and various sports venues such as a tennis court, soccer field, or community pool.

Commercial recreation includescommercialzones where citizens and tourists can go shopping and spend time at the malls and all kinds of cozy boutiques.

title: Citizens
text:
a tennis court, soccer field, or community pool.

Commercial recreation includescommercialzones where citizens and tourists can go shopping and spend time at the malls and all kinds of cozy boutiques.

Citizens can also gain Leisure fromEntertainment, visiting local bars, or eating fancy meals at local restaurants. These Leisure locations differ from others in that the citizens and tourists pay for the Leisure gain as they buy beverages and meals while at the location, combining their need for Goods with their need for Leisure.

Your citizens can also decide to travel to one of theOutside Connectionsfor Leisure essentially becoming tourists in another city. The whole household hops into their car or on one of the public transport options and leaves the city for a few days.

Lastly,Weatheraffects citizens' and tourists’ behavior when choosing Leisure locations. On sunny and warm weather citizens will seek Leisure in outdoors areas such as parks, while on rainy, snowy or cold weather citizens will seek Leisure in indoors areas in the form of shopping and entertainment.

### Health

title: Citizens
text:
her citizens will seek Leisure in outdoors areas such as parks, while on rainy, snowy or cold weather citizens will seek Leisure in indoors areas in the form of shopping and entertainment.

### Health

The physical health of your citizens can be inspected in the Healthcare infoview, which shows the Health of the residential buildings as a whole as well the city’s average Health. If a citizen is sick or injured, it is shown in their info panel. Health is affected by the availability of Healthcare in the form of a passive Health boost received by living in close proximity to a medical facility and receiving treatment at medical facilities in case of a sickness or injury from an accident, both of which severely reduce a citizen’s Health.

Living in areas affected by air and ground pollution has a negative impact on the citizens’ Health. The stronger the pollution is the more severe the effect. If the city has insufficient sewage treatment, it will cause the sewage to back up decreasing citizens’ Health. While garbage piling up affects the citizens’ Well-being, it also affects their Health, so providing the city with adequate waste management is important for the quality of life of the citizens, and if the city isn’t up to the task, the citizens will tell you about it on their Chirper accounts.

title: Citizens
text:
iding the city with adequate waste management is important for the quality of life of the citizens, and if the city isn’t up to the task, the citizens will tell you about it on their Chirper accounts.

Citizens can become sick even if they are not directly affected by any of the aforementioned causes. The probability of becoming sick depends on the Health of the citizen: higher Health reduces the risk of getting sick with a cold. However, when a citizen does become sick, their Health decreases greatly. Sick citizens travel to the nearest medical facility to get treatment for their sickness, but if their condition is too poor to travel by themselves, an ambulance is sent to pick them up.

Citizens also require medical treatment if they get injured in traffic accidents. In that case, an ambulance is sent to the site of a traffic accident to pick up the injured and bring them to the medical facility. Similar to sickness, the citizens get medical treatment while at the hospital or medical clinic.

After the sickness or injury has been treated, the citizen’s Health starts to improve. However, while sick or injured the citizen has a chance of dying. The probability is relatively low in general but is related to the citizen’s current Health level; the lower the Health the higher the risk of dying as a result of their condition.

## Education

title: Citizens
text:
ying. The probability is relatively low in general but is related to the citizen’s current Health level; the lower the Health the higher the risk of dying as a result of their condition.

## Education

Education determines the highest job level a citizen can have and also increase their wages and efficiency and decreases their water and electricity consumption and garbage production. There are 5 levels of education: Uneducated, Poorly Educated, Educated, Well Educated, and Highly Educated. Each school provides one level of education for citizens.

1. Elementary School raises education from Uneducated to Poorly Educated. Only Children can attend. By default, costs 50/month to attend.
2. High School raises education from Poorly Educated to Educated. Only Teens can attend. By default, costs 100/month to attend.
3. College raises education from Educated to Well Educated. Only Teens and Adults can attend. By default, costs 200/month to attend.
4. University raises education from Well Educated to Highly Educated. Only Adults can attend. By default, costs 200/month to attend.

title: Citizens
text:
Teens and Adults can attend. By default, costs 200/month to attend.
4. University raises education from Well Educated to Highly Educated. Only Adults can attend. By default, costs 200/month to attend.

Teens and adults can choose to go to school or get a job. To make this decision they calculate the potential income if they choose to go to work and compare it to the income they will receive from a higher education level job after they graduate from school and see which one is more profitable for them. Some citizens have a preference for working instead of school and even if the potential future income would be better in the long run, they will still choose to work instead of studying.

## Employment

Companies, found in commercial, industrial, and office zones, and most city services have workplaces for citizens to fill. They are divided into complexity levels which reflect the citizen Education levels. City service workplaces remain the same throughout the building’s existence but when a zoned building levels up, the complexity and composition of the available jobs changes. A higher complexity level company requires employees with higher levels of education than a company with a lower complexity level.

title: Citizens
text:
els up, the complexity and composition of the available jobs changes. A higher complexity level company requires employees with higher levels of education than a company with a lower complexity level.

Zoned companies can also change the number of employees needed. If the company detects that they are doing well, they can expand their workforce. If on the other hand, they are doing poorly, they can shrink the workforce and save on wage costs to remain profitable and survive. Between the different company types, industrial manufacturing companies tend to have lower education requirements for employees regardless of building level while commercial and office companies' level of complexity shifts more towards higher educated employees as they level up.

### Work efficiency

Employee Education level represents their capa city to work in different jobs and positions. Higher educated employees are also more efficient in their work and thus they have a more significant influence on the company's Efficiency as well as its production output. When a company is missing employees, the higher education level jobs impact the Efficiency and output more.

title: Citizens
text:
e a more significant influence on the company's Efficiency as well as its production output. When a company is missing employees, the higher education level jobs impact the Efficiency and output more.

Employees are able to do any job at their Education level or lower. Even if a higher educated employee fills a lower level job, their effect on the company’s Efficiency is calculated with the job’s level, not their individual Education level, as they are not able to use all their talents in that position. If the company doesn’t have employees at a high enough Education level, they can’t fill all the available jobs. This results in decreased Efficiency and production.

Citizen Happiness also affects the company's Efficiency and thus its production and profitability. Happy employees are a valuable commodity to a company so it is important to take care of the citizens’ Well-being and Health to ensure that the companies can thrive. If an employee gets sick or injured, they cannot work which means that the company loses some of its Efficiency. Once the employee has gotten well, they return to work life and continue contributing to the company’s Efficiency.

### Wages

title: Citizens
text:
hey cannot work which means that the company loses some of its Efficiency. Once the employee has gotten well, they return to work life and continue contributing to the company’s Efficiency.

### Wages

A citizen working a Job receives a wage in accordance to the citizens education level. There are no other factors in determining a Citizens wage other than their education level, a lack of a certain specialist will not change the salary of employed citizens in said tier of education. The salaries for citizens with different wages goes as follows in 1.3.3f1:

- Highly Educated (Manager): ₡2700/month
- Well Educated (Specialist): ₡2400/month
- Educated (Senior): ₡2100/month
- Poorly Educated (Basic): ₡1800/month*
- Uneducated (Simple): ₡1500/month*

title: Citizens
text:
.3.3f1:

- Highly Educated (Manager): ₡2700/month
- Well Educated (Specialist): ₡2400/month
- Educated (Senior): ₡2100/month
- Poorly Educated (Basic): ₡1800/month*
- Uneducated (Simple): ₡1500/month*

As of 1.3.3f1, these wages are deducted from companies or from the city's budget. Taxes are paid under the Residential category on income above the`Residential Minimum Earnings`of ₡1400/month. City service workers pay tax, but commuters (both people working outside the city and people living outside of the city) do not pay tax at all to either city. Commuters (only people who live outside the city and work in the city) have a`Commuter Wage Multiplier`of 1.1. People who do not work receive financial benefits that are created by the game and are not deducted from any company, household, or municipal balance (presumably paid by the larger government): children receive a`Family Allowance`of ₡400/month for their household, seniors receive a`Pension`of ₡1200/month, and other groups of people that are unemployed receive an`Unemployment Benefit`of ₡800/month up until the`Unemployment Allowance Max Days`of 10.

## Conditions

title: Citizens
text:
eniors receive a`Pension`of ₡1200/month, and other groups of people that are unemployed receive an`Unemployment Benefit`of ₡800/month up until the`Unemployment Allowance Max Days`of 10.

## Conditions

Certain events can cause Conditions for citizens which are visible on the citizen’s info panel. While there is no direct way to remove Conditions from citizens, the player can investigate the causes and through city management help the overall situation. As an example, if citizens are becoming sick it can be a sign of sewage backing up in pipes, pollution spreading to residential areas, or insufficient Healthcare service in the city causing decreased Health which leads to an increased probability of becoming sick. Depending on the cause you can improve the situation by expanding city services, such as your sewage treatment options, or by building a Medical Clinic or a Hospital to take care of the Sick condition and remove the negative effects from the area making sure that the citizens remain healthy. Below we have an overview of the different conditions citizens can be afflicted with.

title: Citizens
text:
of the Sick condition and remove the negative effects from the area making sure that the citizens remain healthy. Below we have an overview of the different conditions citizens can be afflicted with.

- Sick- Sick citizens cannot work. As long as they remain Sick, they are in increased danger of dying. Medical treatment is required to recover from the Sick condition.
- Injured- Injured citizens cannot work. Citizens become injured when involved in traffic accidents, building fires and collapse, and natural disasters. Medical treatment is required to recover from the Injured condition.
- Homeless- Citizens who cannot pay the rent where they live and with no means to move to another home or out of the city become homeless. They have no permanent residence and end up living in the various city parks.
- Malcontent Unwell- Citizens who are Unwell have a low Well-being value. Their Happiness value decreases and their contribution to work is also negatively affected. Additionally, they are at risk of becoming criminals if their Well-being doesn’t increase.
- Weak- Citizens who are Weak have a low Health value. This decreases their Happiness and negatively affects their contribution to work. Citizens with low Health are at a high risk of becoming sick, and when a Weak citizen becomes sick, their Health value decreases to very low putting them at an increased risk of dying if they don’t get medical treatment.
- In Distress Distressed- Citizens who are in Danger or Trapped, are Distressed. Citizens are in Danger when they are in a burning building while Trapped citizens are involved in a building collapse and are buried under the rubble. Sometimes citizens who are in a burning building can also become Trapped, unable to escape.
- Evacuated- Evacuated citizens are in an emergency shelter. Citizens can travel to an emergency shelter by themselves or they can be picked up by an Evacuation Bus which transports them to the emergency shelter.

title: Citizens
text:
Evacuated citizens are in an emergency shelter. Citizens can travel to an emergency shelter by themselves or they can be picked up by an Evacuation Bus which transports them to the emergency shelter.

## Criminals

Citizens can become criminals. The probability of this is relatively low but the chance is increased if the citizen’s Well-being is low. When this happens, the citizen will commit a crime to earn income by first selecting the target of their criminal activity. The target is a building where the robbery takes place and it is selected by comparing the buildings in the city and their crime probability values. The final choice is a random pick from the group of buildings with the highest crime probability values. The citizens living in the building targeted by the criminal will suffer a temporary loss of Well-being.

The criminal will travel to the building, commit the crime there (which takes some time) and then leave. When the crime takes place, an alarm is triggered and alerts the police station, but there is a small delay before the patrol car is sent to the crime scene. If the patrol car reaches the location before the criminal leaves, the criminal is arrested. If the criminal leaves before the police arrive, they escape the law and are free to continue their criminal deeds another day!

title: Citizens
text:
aches the location before the criminal leaves, the criminal is arrested. If the criminal leaves before the police arrive, they escape the law and are free to continue their criminal deeds another day!

When a criminal isarrested, the police take them to a jail located at Police Stations and Police Headquarters. There a portion of the arrested criminals are sentenced to serve time in Prison, either in the city if one is available, or at one of the Outside Connections if one is not available. Citizens who are not sentenced to prison are released and their criminal status is reset just like criminals who have served their time in prison. Once released they rejoin society as regular citizens. Depending on their age, citizens can die of old age while serving their prison sentence.

## Tourists

Tourists are citizens who visit the city briefly. They stay at a local hotel while in the city and make choices daily whether to visit one of the city’s attractions and commercial areas or to stay at the hotel. The city’s Attractiveness value determines how many tourists are drawn to the city and this value can be increased by building parks and various other attractions such as landmarks.

title: Citizens
text:
stay at the hotel. The city’s Attractiveness value determines how many tourists are drawn to the city and this value can be increased by building parks and various other attractions such as landmarks.

Tourists make similar calculations about their Leisure targets as citizens comparing the Leisure value of the locations to their distance from the hotel and deciding which of them is the best option for them to gain Leisure.

## Lifepath

Citizens have a lifepath dotted with different events such as graduating from a school, finding a spouse, landing a new job, and moving to a new home or away from the city. You can keep an eye on these events through the Lifepath by following a citizen. This is done by selecting the citizen and clicking the Follow button, adding them to the list of Followed Citizens found on the right side of the screen right below Chirper.

The Lifepath records everything from the added citizens from the moment they are followed. The journal entry lists their name, home address, current occupation, Happiness, and other information as well as their Chirper feed where they tell the whole world what is going on in their daily life. A keen player can spot the connections between the citizen’s surroundings such as education options, job opportunities, and living conditions to the changes in their lives, the jobs they get, and the choices they make.

title: Climate
text:
# Climate

Source: https://cs2.paradoxwikis.com/Climate

Please help with verifying or updating older sections of this article.
At least some were last verified forversion1.0.

Cities: Skylines IIfeatures different types of climates with various characteristics unique to each map. Climates are based on latitude and longitude resulting in differences in the length of day and amount of sunlight as well as other elements such as temperature range, amount of cloud coverage, and precipitation. Those parameters mean all maps will introduce different Climates with four distinct seasons.

Climates can be ground into three basic Climate types that act as a foundation for each map’s unique characteristics. Maps based on the Temperate Climate have distinct seasonal changes, moderate precipitation, and a wide temperature range, while maps with the Continental Climate type introduce significant annual variations in temperature. Lastly, we have the Polar regions which feature a short and cold summer and a long-lasting cold winter. You may recognize which category a map falls into based on its displayed temperature range, but as Climates are customized for each map, you will experience significant differences between maps that fall into the Continental group.

## Weather

title: Climate
text:
s into based on its displayed temperature range, but as Climates are customized for each map, you will experience significant differences between maps that fall into the Continental group.

## Weather

Weather is closely tied to the climate and represents the climate’s seasonal changes. Each map features unique weather which changes with the simulation. You can encounter various intensities of rainy weather, different cloud coverage, temperature changes, etc.

Temperature has an influence on how much electricity the city consumes. The city consumes the least electricity at temperatures between 18°C/64°F and 22°C/71°F. Above and below those temperatures the electricity consumption increases until it reaches 200% below -18°C/-0.4°F or above 58°C/136°F.

On sunny and warm weather citizens will seek Leisure in outdoors areas such asparks, while on rainy, snowy or cold weather citizens will seek Leisure in indoors areas in the form of shopping and entertainment.

On cloudy weather solar power plants produce 25% less electricity.

## Months and seasons

Each month lasts a day and night cycle and each season lasts three months. The length of the day-night cycle is based on the map’s climate and current season with nights being longer during the winter. As the sun sets the building lights and street lights come on, lighting the way for citizens.

title: Climate
text:
y-night cycle is based on the map’s climate and current season with nights being longer during the winter. As the sun sets the building lights and street lights come on, lighting the way for citizens.

Seasons affect more than just the amount of sunlight in a day though. Depending on the Climate, temperature changes over the course of the whole year, as does the chance of rain and cloud coverage. During cold months snow falls and covers the ground of maps with Climates that include cold seasons. When there is snow, the Road Maintenance building’s snowplows take care of excess snow on the roads, keeping up the road condition to reduce the risk of accidents. The map’s location plays an important role when it comes to seasons as cities located in the southern hemisphere experience the seasons at opposite times of the year compared to the northern hemisphere.

## Natural disasters

title: Climate
text:
an important role when it comes to seasons as cities located in the southern hemisphere experience the seasons at opposite times of the year compared to the northern hemisphere.

## Natural disasters

Beyond the normal weather phenomena, Cities: Skylines II includes natural disasters adding random events and challenging gameplay. Disasters can be switched on and off when starting a new game or loading an existing save. Disasters can affect your city in many ways, and while you can prepare for possible natural disasters withdisaster control systems, they can’t be avoided completely. On the mild end of the spectrum, disasters might cause traffic accidents that lead to traffic jams, which can further affect your services’ ability to respond to the disaster. They cause damage to buildings and citizens directly hit by the disaster, which burdenshealthcare,deathcare, andrescueservices and causes disruptions to the companies or services housed in the affected buildings.

When a disaster happens citizens will try to get toshelters, which cannot be destroyed by disasters. If all shelters are full they will try to escape in the direction opposite of the disaster.

title: Climate
text:
dings.

When a disaster happens citizens will try to get toshelters, which cannot be destroyed by disasters. If all shelters are full they will try to escape in the direction opposite of the disaster.

Natural disasters are logged into theEvent Journalwhich can be found in the right side panel of the main UI. The journal includes past events in chronological order and information about what happened, when it happened, and what outcome the event had.

### Forest fire

Forest fires can happen in all climates though the risk of a forest fire happening is affected by precipitation level and temperature, for example, dry weather has a higher risk of forest fires breaking out. A forest fire near buildings can spread to them causing building fires and wreaking havoc in the city. It is important to take care of forest fires to prevent harm to the city.

You can prepare for forest fires by placingFirewatch Towerson the map. They decrease forest fire probability and firefighters' response time within their range, limiting the fallout if a forest fire should happen. Fire truckscan extinguish a fire when it reaches the road network but placing aFirefighting Helicopter Depotin your city helps to put out the fire in places where fire trucks have no access.

### Hail storm

title: Climate
text:
ruckscan extinguish a fire when it reaches the road network but placing aFirefighting Helicopter Depotin your city helps to put out the fire in places where fire trucks have no access.

### Hail storm

When the weather is cold, but not freezing, there is a possibility of a hail storm occurring. Hail storms cause physical damage to buildings but do not destroy them. The damaged buildings need to be repaired after the storm and the money and resources for those repairs come from the residents taking away from means that would usually go to building upkeep and leveling. A Hail storm can also cause traffic accidents, which of course interrupts the traffic flow. The citizens don’t need to evacuate but they prefer staying indoors during a hail storm.

### Tornado

The Tornado is the most extreme natural disaster included in Cities: Skylines II. It affects citizens, vehicles, and buildings and can cause serious damage to them as it moves through the city. The buildings can get damaged or even destroyed by the Tornado, citizens can get injured or die, and a lot of traffic accidents tend to happen in the area the Tornado hits.

title: Climate
text:
it moves through the city. The buildings can get damaged or even destroyed by the Tornado, citizens can get injured or die, and a lot of traffic accidents tend to happen in the area the Tornado hits.

The best way to prepare for extreme natural disasters is by placingSmall and Large Emergency Sheltersin your city so that they are easily reachable for citizens. The Early Disaster Warning System can spot approaching natural disasters and informs citizens earlier to go to the shelters in time to avoid the disaster. The citizens will run to the shelters and the large emergency shelters feature evacuation buses that are deployed at the same time as the evacuation order. The evacuation buses will plan their path based on population density, prioritizing schools, medical clinics, and hospitals.

Colossal Order(founded in 2009) is a Finnish video game development company headquartered inTampereknown for its Cities in Motion series, and are the developers ofCities: Skylines. The games developed by Colossal Order are published by Paradox Interactive. Colossal Order was founded in 2009 and their games focus primarily on simulations of business and cities.

title: Common ECS Components
text:
# Common ECS Components

Source: https://cs2.paradoxwikis.com/Common_ECS_Components

WIP document, content needed. Component descriptions may need updating or are unfilled. Feel free to add any improvements to descriptions, or fill in missing ones.

This document assumes the reader has knowledge of Unity ECS fundamentals. Please visitECS - Entity Component Systemfor more information.

There are ECS components used throughout Cities: Skylines systems that are key to understanding the lifecycle of entities within the game. ThisCommunity-Made Guideaims to give an overview of these components.

## Common Components Overview

The components we'll be discussing in this section are located in the`Game. Common`namespace. Those components cleaned at the end of each frame by the`CleanUpSystem`are marked as such. The`CleanUpSystem`and`PrepareCleanUpSystem`are explained in the"Clean Up" Systems.

ComponentTypeCleaned Up?DescriptionProperties

NameTypeDescription

AppliedComponentYes

BatchesUpdatedComponentYesAdded to entities where graphics need to be updated.

CreatedComponentYesAdded to entities that are newly created.

DeletedComponentYes*Added to entities that have recently been deleted or should be deleted. For example, the`HouseholdAndCitizenRemoveSystem`will:

title: Common ECS Components
text:
omponentYesAdded to entities that are newly created.

DeletedComponentYes*Added to entities that have recently been deleted or should be deleted. For example, the`HouseholdAndCitizenRemoveSystem`will:

- mark the residents of a household and their possessions (cars) for deletion
- remove the household as a renter for their residence

*Note:Only cleaned up if there's an`Event`component attached. The`CleanUpSystem`destroys the associated entity.

DestroyedComponentNoAdded to entities that represent destroyed structures. For example, a collapsed building.

EffectsUpdatedComponentYesAdded to entities where the updated visual effects of the object need to be rendered

EventComponentNo

NativeComponentNo

OverriddenComponentNoApplied generally to objects that are conflicting with other objects or networks but are not permanently deleted or removed. They are not shown unless`ShowMarkers`is enabled via the DevUI under Gameplay. Trees and wild bushes will quickly revert to child size when overridden. If the conflicting object or network is removed the overridden component will be removed to reveal the object.

OwnerComponentNoUsed to denote the owner of an entity. For example, the entity representing the citizen that owns an automobile.`m_Owner``Entity`The owner of the specific object.

PathfindUpdatedComponentNoAdded when the path finding parameters for a road lane have been updated.

title: Common ECS Components
text:
representing the citizen that owns an automobile.`m_Owner``Entity`The owner of the specific object.

PathfindUpdatedComponentNoAdded when the path finding parameters for a road lane have been updated.

PseudoRandomSeedComponentNoSome entities have random seeds attached to them for event generation. This component contains that seed and uses it to generate an instance ofUnity. Mathematics. Random. For example, lights in the game use this component to determine when to turn on, and how bright they should be. There are`static readonly`properties (labeled as "reasons") in this component that relate to how numbers should be generated for different scenarios. These numbers are passed to the components`GetRandom`method. These properties are in the collapsed list below:

UpdatedComponentYesApplied to entities that have had components changed other than just a visual change. Including: Transform position changes, overrriden, CullingInfo, Leveling Up, Attached to roads, etc. Updates trigger all subelements to be destroyed and recreated.

## "Clean Up" Systems

Each frame, the`CleanUpSystem`and`PrepareCleanUpSystem`run and remove specific components.

### PrepareCleanUpSystem

The`PrepareCleanUpSystem`runs right after the`MainLoop`system update phase (seeSystem Update Phases). It marks any entity with both`Deleted`and`Event`components for cleanup, and marks any remaining cleanup components in the table above.

### CleanUpSystem

The`CleanUpSystem`runs during the`CleanUp`system update phase (which is after the MainLoop and UI update phase). It takes all marked "Deleted&Event" entities for and deletes them. Other marked components are simply removed.

# Commonly units in the game

Source: https://cs2.paradoxwikis.com/Commonly_units_in_the_game

## Units

In the simulation system we will use many units, some of them are very important and an attempt will be made to document them here.

### Time`:uint`

alias`frameIndex`or`UpdateInterval`

When used in the update interval, it must be a power of 2. For one minute, the value is approximately equal to`182.044444444444444444`.

90 minutes equal to`16384`.

# Creating a Tool

Source: https://cs2.paradoxwikis.com/Creating_a_Tool

Ambox warning blue construction This article is undergoing a majorreconstruction.
You are welcome to assist in its overhaul.

The purpose of thisCommunity-Made Guideis to introduce users to the basics of creating a tool mod in Cities II.

## Definition

A tool in Cities: Skylines II refers to systems that allow the player to directly manipulate and access attributes of their city for a desired result. Examples of tools would be the Bulldoze Tool, Zone Tool, and Selection Tool.

## The Tool Lifecycle

The lifecycle of a tool is managed by`Game. Tools. ToolSystem`. The Game. Tools. DefaultToolSystem is active on simulation startup and when no other tool is running.

[diagram on the lifecycle of a tool goes here]

## The ToolBaseSystem

All tools implement the`ToolBaseSystem`abstract class and are run during`SystemUpdatePhase. ToolUpdate`.

### Lifecycle Methods

#### System Creation

`protected override void OnCreate()`

Just like other systems, the`OnCreate`method is used to initialize any needed data. For tool systems, this is a good place to:

- Set up keyboard hotkeys (no standards have been set for this yet)
- Get reference to input actions from the`InputManager`

Important Note:Youmustset`Enabled`to`false`, or you'll end up overwriting the Default tool.

text:
# Creating and sharing intersection assets

Source: https://cs2.paradoxwikis.com/Creating_and_sharing_intersection_assets

This is acommunity-made guidethat covers how to make and share custom made intersection assets inCities Skylines II.

## Getting started

First step is to open theEditorfrom the main menu of the game. It is recommended tonot use modsas these may cause unexpected results when used to make assets like this in the editor. For an intersection asset it is recommended to start by first creating adouplicate of an existing intersection asset. This can be done through theAsset Browserwindow, which is accessed by left clicking on the folder with the magnifying glass icon. Proceed by searching for"intersection"in the search bar at the top of the window. Click on thethree dots [...]next to any of the vanilla intersection assets and selectDouplicate and place. It does not matter much which one is chosen. It is recommended that the user chooses an intersection that is as close to the desired design as possible as this will let them reuse more of the included roads. Proceed with placing the intersection in the editor space and press escape. There should now be a copy of the selected intersection that can be used as a template for the new intersection asset.

title: Creating and sharing intersection assets
text:
oads. Proceed with placing the intersection in the editor space and press escape. There should now be a copy of the selected intersection that can be used as a template for the new intersection asset.

Now hover the mouse over the intersection and left click on it. This will highlight the intersection underObjectsin theWorkspacewindow. It will also open a asset properties window. The first part of this window has the headerAsset Stamp Prefab. There is no need to manually setConstruction CostorUp Keep Costas these will be calculated automatically. The user may however consider changing theNameof the asset and the asset stamp size by editing theWidthandDepthvalues. These values chiefly matter while while in the editor and centering the asset is not required. A large asset stamp or footprint in the editor will not affect placement of the intersection in-game. Only objects contained within the asset stamp are placed when the intersection is placed in-game. These values can be edited at any time so the footprint can be made larger or smaller later in the creation process.

title: Creating and sharing intersection assets
text:
ontained within the asset stamp are placed when the intersection is placed in-game. These values can be edited at any time so the footprint can be made larger or smaller later in the creation process.

Under theUI Objectheader the user can now select theGroup. By default it should sayRoadIntersections. This may determine where to find the finished intersection in-game. It is recommended not changing the group value. Under this header there is also the option to select an icon image for the intersection asset. Click on the template picture and navigate to a prepared image to use. It is recommended to use a square .png or .jpg image with relatively low resolution for this icon image. There are two further headers to the properties window namelyObject Sub NetsandUnlockable. The options options under these headers can be edited but it is recommended to keep the default values. Advanced users might find use for theAdd componentoption at the bottom of this window but it will not be required for most intersection assets.

## Designing the intersection

title: Creating and sharing intersection assets
text:
keep the default values. Advanced users might find use for theAdd componentoption at the bottom of this window but it will not be required for most intersection assets.

## Designing the intersection

Now it is time to create the roads that make up the intersection. The user should first go into theBulldozetool by clicking on the icon or hitting the hotkey (B by default). ForTool Modethey should mostly be usingSub Elementwhen deleting individual segments of the intersection. Select this mode and delete all the roads of the template intersection that are not required. The intersection is automatically saved after each edit temporarily and will remain in the game for the current session. If the user accidentally deletes the whole thing by using the wrong bulldozer tool mode they can still find and place your intersection again from the asset browser.

Bindsoverlappingscreenshot

title: Creating and sharing intersection assets
text:
ssion. If the user accidentally deletes the whole thing by using the wrong bulldozer tool mode they can still find and place your intersection again from the asset browser.

Bindsoverlappingscreenshot

The asset browser is also how one finds roads to add to the intersection asset. Use the search feature to find the roads, bridges, or other objects that are required. Alternatively one can use theFiltersto filter out objects based on category. When selecting a road for the first time enable theSnappingoptionBinds overlapping items to a building. This proper snapping setting is vital for this guide so make sure to find and enable it. This option is only available in the editor. With it turned on any roads created inside the asset stamp area should automatically be added to the intersection asset. Now proceed with building the intersection in the same way as one would while playing the game. Not every in-game option appears to be viable in the editor and there are limitations in how roads will interact with the terrain. Ground level roads and elevated roads supported on pillars as well as retaining walls work adequately in most situations. TheReplacetool mode works as in the regular game and road upgrades such as roundabouts, turn restrictions, traffic lights, etc. should also be available and persistent when used in the editor. Road upgrades are also found using the asset browser.

title: Creating and sharing intersection assets
text:
me and road upgrades such as roundabouts, turn restrictions, traffic lights, etc. should also be available and persistent when used in the editor. Road upgrades are also found using the asset browser.

Sometimes roads will not be added to the asset even with the proper snapping options enabled. Before placement of a segment one should see the green grid of the asset stamp. This means the road will be added to the asset. To check if roads already placed are included in the asset the user can select the bulldozer tool with theMain Elementtool mode and carefully hover over over the roads. If everything appears highlighted together all is good but if certain parts are highlighted separately those are probably not part of the asset. The best way to deal with these is to bulldoze them carefully and redraw them. Be extra cautions and make sure to see the green asset stamp grid while finalizing the placement.

## Saving and testing the asset

Once the intersection is finished it is time to save the asset as a file and test it in game. In the workspace window under objects, or the asset browser, click on the three dots [...] and selectSave. In theSave Assetwindow that pops up select aFile Nameand hitSave Asset. The asset is now stored as local files in theImportedDatafolder which can be found at the following file directory:

title: Creating and sharing intersection assets
text:
selectSave. In theSave Assetwindow that pops up select aFile Nameand hitSave Asset. The asset is now stored as local files in theImportedDatafolder which can be found at the following file directory:

%USERPROFILE%\AppData\LocalLow\Colossal Order\Cities Skylines II\ImportedData

title: Creating and sharing intersection assets
text:
sset. The asset is now stored as local files in theImportedDatafolder which can be found at the following file directory:

%USERPROFILE%\AppData\LocalLow\Colossal Order\Cities Skylines II\ImportedData

Now head back to the main menu and open a game session by either loading an existing save or starting a new city with unlock all enabled. Find the intersection and place it. If one has followed the guide the intersection asset should appear in the intersection tab of the roads menu. Try placing the intersection without anarchy mods enabled. Sometimes roads do not properly connect even if they appear connected in the editor and this may prevent one from placing the intersection in-game with anOverlapping itemstool tip showing up. Take note of issues like this and any other unwanted imperfections or inconsistencies. Load back into the editor, find the asset in the asset browser, and place it in the editor space again. When re-opening the asset in the editor some roads may appear slightly different from how the appeared when first created. This will be more notable for segments at or near the transition between ground and elevated segments. These may as a consequence also appear morejankyfor lack of a better word. This does not always mean the work is lost and often the transitions will remain smooth when the intersection is placed in-game regardless of how it may appear in the editor. Redraw segments that manifested broken connections as noted during the in-game test placement. Once satisfied save the asset again and repeat the in-game test placement. When satisfied with testing the intersection asset is finished.

title: Creating and sharing intersection assets
text:
roken connections as noted during the in-game test placement. Once satisfied save the asset again and repeat the in-game test placement. When satisfied with testing the intersection asset is finished.

## Sharing to Paradox Mods

Sharing to Paradox Mods is completely voluntary and not required to use the intersection asset for personal use. In the editor workspace window under objects, or the asset browser, click on the three dots [...] and selectShare. At the bottom of the window that pops up selectBuild Package. Advanced users may be able to use this window in order toAdd Assets. In theUpload Assetwindow that now pops up one can set theAsset Name, which will be the name of the asset on Paradox Mods. The user is also given the opportunity to provide aShort Description, aLong Description, aForum Link,External Links,Screenshots,Additional tags, andAdditional assets. There is no requirement to add all of this right now as they can be added or edited later on the Paradox Mods web page. It is however recommended toprovide a thumbnail right awayas there may be some cookie related issues where users that have once viewed the upload will not be able to see updated thumbnails unless they clear cookies on their end.

title: Creating and sharing intersection assets
text:
provide a thumbnail right awayas there may be some cookie related issues where users that have once viewed the upload will not be able to see updated thumbnails unless they clear cookies on their end.

Thumbnails should can be .png or .jpg files and have a square aspect ratio. To add a thumbnail left click on the image at the top of the window that is just underneath the Upload Asset window header text. The menu will open to a specific directory in the game files so if the user has a prepared thumbnail image they should place it here:

%USERPROFILE%\AppData\LocalLow\Colossal Order\Cities Skylines II\Screenshots

Back in the Upload Asset window there is also aVisibilityoption. Setting this to Public means anyone can access the asset page from Paradox Mods. Setting it to Unlisted means that anyone with the link can access it. Setting visibility to Private means that only you can access the asset page which can be useful if you want to keep editing before making it public via the web page edit menu. Finally click on theSubmitbutton to upload the intersection asset to Paradox Mods. The asset page should now soon appear inthe users list of uploads.

## Further tips

title: Creating and sharing intersection assets
text:
ublic via the web page edit menu. Finally click on theSubmitbutton to upload the intersection asset to Paradox Mods. The asset page should now soon appear inthe users list of uploads.

## Further tips

- One can hide the advisor window by clicking on the UI icon with the student/graduate hat.
- In the asset browser, hover just left of the three dots of roads, or other objects, and click on the star that appears. This adds the road to a favorites list and will make it much faster to find when opening the browser without filters or searching.
- Create many douplicates and backup copies for if the need arrises to revert to an earlier version of the intersection.
- Be consistent and strucured when naming assets and douplicates.
- The Cities Skylines II editor allows parallel creation of multiple assets side-by-side in the same editor space.
- Using the editor grid as a guide and enabling theSnap to lot gridsnapping setting can be very useful for creating symmetric designs.

# Creating UI And Code Mods

Source: https://cs2.paradoxwikis.com/Creating_UI_And_Code_Mods

Please help with verifying or updating older sections of this article.
At least some were last verified forversion1.1.

## Creating The Projects

Be sure to check theModding Toolchainguide to first get familiar with the modding setup in Cities: Skylines II.

### Create your Code Mod

You can do so using the template provided by the game:

### Create a UI Mod alongside your Code Mod

Open the command prompt inside the folder that contains your Code mod's`.csproj`file, then create the UI Mod using the`npx create-csii-ui-mod`command.

## Configuring Your Mods

### UI Mod

In your UI Mod's`mod.json`file, make sure your`id`matches your Code mod's target name.

Which should look like this:

This allows you to build your UI mod on its own to benefit from the hot reload, as well as building it directly with the code part of your mod.

### Building the UI Mod with the Code Mod

To streamline the build operation, you'll want to build both UI and Code mods at the same time.

To do that, you'll need to add the following to your Code Mod's project (`.csproj`) file.

```
<Target Name="BuildUI" AfterTargets="AfterBuild">
 <Exec Command="npm run build" WorkingDirectory="$(ProjectDir)/MyUIMod" />
</Target>

```

Be sure to use the correct name of your UI mod in the working directory value.

## Results

title: Creating UI And Code Mods
text:
