 <Exec Command="npm run build" WorkingDirectory="$(ProjectDir)/MyUIMod" />
</Target>

```

Be sure to use the correct name of your UI mod in the working directory value.

## Results

This concludes our UI+Code guide. Your mod will now handle all of Code and UI build processes in one click, as well streamlining the publish process to PDX Mods.

title: Debugging
text:
# Debugging

Source: https://cs2.paradoxwikis.com/Debugging

This Community-Made Guide will help you setup a Cities: Skylines II installation that supports real-time .NET debugging.

This allows you to place breakpoints and have debug insights within your IDE.

## Enable Unity Support in IDE

- Visual Studio: You will need to have the Unity Game Development workload installed via the Visual Studio Installer.
- Rider: Enable Unity Support bundled plugin if it's not already enabled.

## Automated debugging

Reference the CS2-ModdingTools NuGet package from your modding project to automatically enable debugging with Unity. It automatically executes the steps below when a mod is compiled in Debug mode, and it can also automatically revert them when a Clean or Release build is triggered.

WARNING: Since the patch v1.5.7f1 from end of april 2026 and with the 1.0.5 version of this NuGet package, you may have a "Failed to load PlayerSettings (internal index#0)" error because of unity version mismatch when starting the game. Thus remove this package from your dependencies and follow the manual path for now. Please remove this warning when the package get updated.

## Modifying the game files to support a debugger

You will likely have to repeat those actions every time a game update is released, in particular if the game uses a new version of Unity.

title: Debugging
section: Modifying the game files to support a debugger
text:
t updated.

## Modifying the game files to support a debugger

You will likely have to repeat those actions every time a game update is released, in particular if the game uses a new version of Unity.

1. Find the Unity Version the game is currently using by navigating to the Cities: Skylines II Game directory (`C:\Program Files (x86)\Steam\steamapps\common\Cities Skylines II`) and right clicking on `Cities2.exe`. The Unity Editor Version can be found under "Product Version"
2. Find your Unity Editor install directory (Ex. `C:\Program Files\Unity 2022.X.XXf1`, exact version may vary) and navigate to the subdirectory `\Editor\Data\PlaybackEngines\windowsstandalonesupport\Variations\win64_player_development_mono`.
Alternatively, if using Unity Hub, go to Installs page, then "Manage" the version appropriate for the current game version, and select "Show in Explorer". This will open up the `Editor` folder mentioned in the path above. Browse to the `win64_player_development_mono`.
3. Copy the `UnityPlayer.dll` in the `win64_player_development_mono` folder to the game directory (not the AppData one, but the actual game install, ex. via Steam `C:\Program Files (x86)\Steam\steamapps\common\Cities Skylines II`).
4. On the same directory, inside `Cities2_Data` folder, open `boot.config` in any text editor.
5. Add the line `player-connection-debug=1` to the bottom of the file.

title: Debugging
section: Modifying the game files to support a debugger
text:
mapps\common\Cities Skylines II`).
4. On the same directory, inside `Cities2_Data` folder, open `boot.config` in any text editor.
5. Add the line `player-connection-debug=1` to the bottom of the file.

Now when you start the game in any way, the game should show a small "Development Build" label in the bottom-right corner if everything went right.

## Debugging

When the game window opens, you can attach the debugger like so:

### Visual Studio

1. Debug → Attach Unity Debugger
2. Select the (hopefully only one) process shown and click Attach
3. Your breakpoints should be resolved and be hit, as long as your mod is locally installed.

### Rider

1. Run → Attach to Unity Process...
2. Select the (hopefully only one) process shown and click OK
3. Your breakpoints should be resolved (little tick mark on breakpoints when your mod is loaded) and be hit, as long as your mod is locally installed.

Drift into a world of tranquility with Deluxe Relax Station, where ambient melodies and soothing soundscapes gently envelop you. CS2 ForumParadox Mods is our own platform-neutral modding system. Paradox’s considered opinion is that mods are, and should always be, free of charge community-created content. This is not something that we will ever change.2023-10-19

title: Developer mode
text:
# Developer mode

Source: https://cs2.paradoxwikis.com/Developer_mode

This article istimelessand should be accurate for any version of the game.

Alert building on fire Warning: Danger ahead
Using developer mode has the potential to crash your game and break your saves. Some of its options can't be undone. Frequent backups are strongly recommended.

This is aCommunity-Made Guide. Cities: Skylines IIfeatures a developer menu that can be used to manipulate the game.

## Enabling developer mode

On Steam:

1. Right-click the game, open Properties
2. Add`-developerMode`to the Launch Options at the bottom

On Xbox GamePass:

The method can be applied on Paradox Launcher or Cities: Skylines II executables.

1. Game executable is located in the following directory:`YourDrive:\XboxGames\Cities- Skylines II - PC Edition\Content\Cities2.exe`
2. Create a shortcut of the executable (to a directory of your choice)
3. Right-click the shortcut, open Properties.
4. In Shorcut tab, add`-developerMode`right after the executable location in "Target". The end result should look like this:`"YourDrive:\XboxGames\Cities- Skylines II - PC Edition\Content\Cities2.exe" -developerMode`
5. Use the shortcut to launch the game with Developer Mode.

title: Developer mode
text:
"Target". The end result should look like this:`"YourDrive:\XboxGames\Cities- Skylines II - PC Edition\Content\Cities2.exe" -developerMode`
5. Use the shortcut to launch the game with Developer Mode.

Should you wish to use Paradox Launcher to launch the game, you can find the executable in the following directory`YourDrive:\XboxGames\Cities- Skylines II - PC Edition\Content\Launcher\bin\Paradox Launcher.exe`

## Using developer mode

Once enabled, the developer menu is opened by pressing⇆Tab. The Simulation tab contains various cheats such as instant zone development, disabling pollution, or disabling the need for services. Spawning events and disasters requires selecting a building beforehand. Checking "bypass validation results" disables checks before placing buildings and networks and can be used to create quays easier closer to the waterline.

The add object menu is opened by pressing🏠 Home. It lets you access many more objects than those available through the default user interface.

## Known Issues

Developer mode is not intended to be used for gameplay purposes. There have been reported issues following the use of developer mode such as:

- Inability to remove certain type of objects following their placement.
- Inability to re-open the Object Tab after closing via dev-UI interface.
- Broken road sections/junctions.

Workarounds are possible for some of these issues;

title: Developer mode
text:
pe of objects following their placement.
- Inability to re-open the Object Tab after closing via dev-UI interface.
- Broken road sections/junctions.

Workarounds are possible for some of these issues;

1. Users should avoid placing any objects such as; cims, vehicles, animals. If placed, vehicles cannot be removed later by using bulldozer tool. In order to remove them, users will need use "Remove Residents/Vehicles" option, located in dev-UI menu->Simulation tab. The option will temporarily despawn all residents and vehicles.
2. Do not close the Object List tab via the interface, but toggle it with🏠 Homekey instead.
3. Avoid using Bypass mode for networks if possible, especially on complex junctions.

# Development Tree

Source: https://cs2.paradoxwikis.com/Development_Tree

Development Treeallow players to access new service buildings and other buildable items for your city. Players can unlock these assets by using development points. Development points are awarded for reaching newmilestones. Each City Service has its own development tree. Players can focus on specific types of city development by spending their points in the areas that interest them the most.

title: Districts
text:
# Districts

Source: https://cs2.paradoxwikis.com/Districts

Ambox stub This article is aStub, which means that it does not sufficiently cover its subject matter.
Please help expandthisarticle if you can.

Please help with verifying or updating older sections of this article.
At least some were last verified forversion1.0.

Districts give you more in-depth information about thepopulaceresiding there, whilepoliciescan be applied to individual districts or city-wide bringing flavor and fine-tuning to different areas of the city. The District Panel shows information about how many citizens live there, their averagewealthlevel and what are theirEducationlevels, as well as which policies are enabled for the district. Hovering over theHappinessfor the district will show you all the benefits and drawbacks of the area, giving you an idea of how you can improve that part of the city.

Creating a district is quite straightforward. With the District Tool, you define an area by placing corner nodes and once you “close” the area, the district is created. This method is faster and more precise than in the game’s predecessor, and the district can be adjusted after it’s created by moving the nodes or adding new nodes to the edges. It’s named automatically but you are able to rename it after its creation to suit your city.

title: Districts
text:
, and the district can be adjusted after it’s created by moving the nodes or adding new nodes to the edges. It’s named automatically but you are able to rename it after its creation to suit your city.

Cityservicescan be assigned to one or more districts limiting their services to only those areas of the city. Services that have not been assigned to any districts will service the entire city, regardless of district borders. It is important to note that even though a service building is assigned to multiple districts, its passive service coverage effect still applies only to its neighborhood. Only its simulated service effects i.e. its vehicles travel to all of the assigned districts.

## See also

- District policies

title: Dragon Gate
text:
# Dragon Gate

Source: https://cs2.paradoxwikis.com/Dragon_Gate

This article istimelessand should be accurate for any version of the game.

Dragon Gate Dragon Gate

DLC banner Dragon Gate Type

Content Creator Pack Release date

2025-03-18Store: Content Creator Pack

Paradox/Steam

TheDragon GateContent Creator Pack was announced on 2025-03-10[1], alongside theLeisure VenuesandMediterranean HeritageCCPs, as well as theAtmospheric Piano Channel,Feelgood Funk RadioandJade Road Radiomusic packs. All were released on 2025-03-18 alongsidePatch 1.2.5f1(aka theAnniversary Patch).

## Official description

With a blend of tradition and modernity, this Creator Pack by community creator ‘Emperor Li’ allows you to create a vibrant Chinese inspired market quarter. Let your citizens take a stroll down shopping streets adorned with classic elements such as lanterns and shop signs, or visit the traditional temple that adds a touch of East Asian splendor to your cityscape. With vibrant red and gold, ‘Dragon Gate’ will bring the fiery charm and elegance of Chinese architecture to your city.

### Low Density Business Zone

Create a bustling market quarter infused with iconic Chinese architecture with zoneable commercial low-density buildings. The assets are divided into various lot sizes across five levels - all contributing their unique character to your city.

### Parks

title: Dragon Gate
text:
Chinese architecture with zoneable commercial low-density buildings. The assets are divided into various lot sizes across five levels - all contributing their unique character to your city.

### Parks

Traditional architecture in China, and iconic around the world, these will pique the interests of citizens and tourists alike.

- Small Chinese Gate
- Large Chinese Gate
- Traditional Chinese Temple

### Signature Building

Combining Chinese and Western style to secure plenty of accommodations for tourists and business travelers.

- Grand Dynasty Hotel

### Service Building

Advanced education in a historical building, a school for teenagers that can be upgraded with an extension wing.

- Chinese High School

title: Economy
text:
# Economy

Source: https://cs2.paradoxwikis.com/Economy

Please help with verifying or updating older sections of this article.
At least some were last verified forversion1.0.

Economyis a menu that shows information about the city's income and expenses and allows the player to adjust taxes and budget and take loans. The panel can easily be found by pressing the icon that looks like stacked green dollars. From the Budget tab, you can take a quick glance at the city’s Revenue, Expenses, and its current Monthly Balance. Additionally, highlighting each section reveals quick details of each of the revenues or expenses on the right side of the panel. It includes a description as well as an overview of where the revenue or expenses come from.

All cities will initially receive a limited amount of income. The higher the income from other sources, the lower the government subsidies income will be, until it will eventually stop.

## Taxation

Taxation is unlocked at 2. Each of the zone types has its own overall tax rate which can be adjusted with sliders. On top of that, you can adjust the taxes of individual groups by expanding the menu for a specific zone type. Each tax can be set to be between -10% and 30%.

Lower taxes help buildings level up faster while higher taxes bring more money.

title: Economy
text:
es of individual groups by expanding the menu for a specific zone type. Each tax can be set to be between -10% and 30%.

Lower taxes help buildings level up faster while higher taxes bring more money.

Residential taxes are collected from the citizens' monthly wages. Lower taxes will increase demand for residential zones while higher taxes will lower it. Taxes can be set separately for each Education Level.

Commercial, Industrial and Office taxes are collected from the companies' monthly profits. Taxes can be set separately for each product type. Lowering taxes for certain products and increasing them for others is an effective way to specialize the city in a certain field.

## Service Fees

Service fees may be collected for the following services, offsetting costs or even being profitable:

- Roads, in the form of parking fees, which can be adjusted between ₡0 and ₡50.
- Electricity, which is 0.2 by default.
- Water, which is 0.3 by default.
- Healthcare, which is ₡100/month/hospitalized citizen by default.
- Garbage, which is 1.0 by default.
- Education, which, by default, is ₡50/month/citizen for elementary school students, ₡100/month/citizen for high school students, and ₡200/month/citizen for college and university students.
- Transportation, from fares.

## Service Trade Main article:Services#Service_trade

The following services can be exported or imported:

title: Economy
text:
ts, and ₡200/month/citizen for college and university students.
- Transportation, from fares.

## Service Trade Main article:Services#Service_trade

The following services can be exported or imported:

- Electricity, imported for ₡5000/MW and exported for ₡2500/MW.
- Water, imported for approximately ₡0.1/m3and exported for approximately ₡0.05/m3.
- Sewage, exported for approximately ₡0.1/m3(and unable to be imported).
- Police, imported for ₡50/citizen.
- Healthcare, imported for ₡25/citizen.
- Deathcare, imported for ₡25/citizen.
- Fire, imported for ₡50/citizen.
- Garbage, imported for ₡5/citizen.

## Loans

Loans are large sums of money that a city can take at the cost of adding up to 2.3-20% of the loaned amount to monthly expenses. The loan can be increased or paid partially or in full at any point. The borrowing limit of the loan depends on the current Milestone, ranging from ₡100,000 to ₡26,100,000. The interest rate can be reduced by 1% by building the City Hall and by 2% by building the Central Bank.

The loans tab is not visible in games with unlimited money.

## Production

title: Economy
text:
000 to ₡26,100,000. The interest rate can be reduced by 1% by building the City Hall and by 2% by building the Central Bank.

The loans tab is not visible in games with unlimited money.

## Production

From the Production tab you can observe each of the Materials, Material Goods, and Immaterial Goods your city is producing, and from that you can also observe whether your city’s production of the resource is either in Surplus or in Deficit. You can highlight each resource to reveal additional information which shows you where the resource can be gained from and where it can be either sold or used. For example, Wood can be produced from Forestry or it can be imported. Wood is also used for Heating and it can further be sold to Processing companies or be Exported to Outside Connections.

All resources will be imported if there is a deficit and exported if there is a surplus.

### Companies

title: Economy
text:
ating and it can further be sold to Processing companies or be Exported to Outside Connections.

All resources will be imported if there is a deficit and exported if there is a surplus.

### Companies

Cities Skylines 2 introduces the concept of companies[1]. These virtual entities come with their own logos and colour-schemes, as well as a focus on a certain material type. For example,Grün Kuhis a food company, specialising in manufacturing foodstuffs, whereasSoft Endsis a toilet paper company, and will convert the processed good Timber into Paper. For material good producers, you will see these companies move into your industrial building to meet demand, or move out if demand for their particular resource type dwindles. While companies will import goods from external sources, it may be beneficial both financially and traffic-wise to try and zone/demolish buildings nearer to certain specialised industries to improve traffic and processing[citation needed].

Immaterial good producers will move into Office buildings, and consumer companies will also move into commercial buildings, with their individual focuses and importing their specific goods, selling these to consumers.

### Efficiency

title: ECS - Entity Component System
text:
# ECS - Entity Component System

Source: https://cs2.paradoxwikis.com/ECS_-_Entity_Component_System

Please help with verifying or updating older sections of this article.
At least some were last verified forversion1.0.

## Definitions

Be sure to checkUnity's documentationon ECS concepts alongside this guide!

### Entities

Think of entities as the basic empty boxes in a game. They're like containers that hold information (components) about things in the game. Unity uses entities to organize data efficiently, making games run smoothly.

For example, what makes a simpleentitybecome abuildingis not any specific definition of the entity, there's no "entity type" or anything like that. A building entity, specifically in CS2, is characterized by having a specific set ofComponents, such as`Building`,`Transform`,`Lot`,`PrefabRef`, amongst some other more specific components. These sets of components are what let the game's systems know what to do with an entity.

### Components

title: ECS - Entity Component System
text:
, such as`Building`,`Transform`,`Lot`,`PrefabRef`, amongst some other more specific components. These sets of components are what let the game's systems know what to do with an entity.

### Components

Components in Unity are like pieces of information that define specific properties or behaviors of entities. They serve as building blocks for entities. Each component holds a particular aspect of data, such as position, speed, or consumption. By combining different components, you can create diverse and interactive game elements. The separation of data into components allows for efficient organization and streamlined processing, which contributes to improved game performance and scalability.

All components should be`struct`data types.

#### Standard Components

The standard components are types that implement the`IComponentData`interface. They can optionally have data fields that hold information about an entity which they're attached to.

- A component can be attached only once per entity.
- An attached component can be removed from an entity.
- An attached component can be updated to change its values

#### Shared Components

Shared components are types that implement the`ISharedComponentData`interface. They behave similarly to the standard components, though are saved differently in thearchetype chunks.

Please refer tothe unity docsfor more information.

title: ECS - Entity Component System
text:
implement the`ISharedComponentData`interface. They behave similarly to the standard components, though are saved differently in thearchetype chunks.

Please refer tothe unity docsfor more information.

#### Buffer Components

Buffer components are types that implement the`IBufferElementData`interface. Buffers are a different type of Component that holds arrays or sequences of data. Contrary to a standard component which you can only attach once per entity.

Think of Buffers as a dynamic list of components. A great example of buffers in the game is`Game. Economy. Resources`. It is used to store how much of each resource an entity (ex. an industrial building) has. There are no restrictions on how many elements are in the Buffer array attached to an entity. This means that a building in our example can have 0, 2, or even 40 elements in the list, depending on how the game, or a mod, manipulates the Buffer.

### Systems

Systems are like managers that handle specific functions in the game. They determine how different pieces of data (components) interact with each other.

Systems are an integral part of Cities: Skylines II, most of what happens in the game is done through systems, and Mods can create systems of their own. Be sure to check theSystemsguide for a more in-depth view of them.

## Coming from CS1 to ECS

title: ECS - Entity Component System
text:
es II, most of what happens in the game is done through systems, and Mods can create systems of their own. Be sure to check theSystemsguide for a more in-depth view of them.

## Coming from CS1 to ECS

Previously, data and information about game objects were saved directly in the objects/classes themselves, this made customizability with mods harder and limited how fast the engine could work with all that information, especially in a simulation game where a lot is happening everywhere. Not to mention how it resulted in classes doing way too many things at once, which is a bad coding practice.

Now, with ECS, data and information are split up into small bits, aka"Components". Those components are then attached to entities which then shape how the entity interacts with the game.

title: ECS - Entity Component System
text:
ng practice.

Now, with ECS, data and information are split up into small bits, aka"Components". Those components are then attached to entities which then shape how the entity interacts with the game.

For example, if you want your custom object to have a location in the world, previously you would add the {x, y, z} coordinates directly in the object's class. Well, now you can just use the`Transform`component provided by the game, which is a`struct`, and attach it to any entity in the game, allowing the component to hold that information for the entity so it's used by systems that utilize the component. Another example when modding existing entities is attaching a custom component to an entity. That custom component can hold any information that your mod needs, like the default value of the electricity consumption of that entity. This example would allow you to keep track of the base value in order to apply a percentage change to the "base" value without losing it when you apply your edits to the`ElectricityConsumer`component's data.

## Archetypes

Archetypes are templates or blueprints for entities. They represent a specific combination of component types that entities can have. Archetypes help Unity optimize memory and processing by grouping entities with similar component compositions together.

title: ECS - Entity Component System
text:
They represent a specific combination of component types that entities can have. Archetypes help Unity optimize memory and processing by grouping entities with similar component compositions together.

When you create an entity in ECS, Unity assigns it to an archetype based on its components. Entities with the same archetype share a similar set of components, making it more efficient to process them collectively. This grouping allows Unity to perform parallelized operations on entities that have identical data structures, improving overall system performance.

In essence, archetypes in Unity ECS are a way of categorizing entities based on their component composition, facilitating more efficient data processing and memory utilization in the game engine.

Archetypes are stored using chunks, aka`ArchetypeChunk`, a 16kb block of data that contains arrays of components, this set of components is what makes an "Archetype". Entities are basically one index in the table of components in that chunk.

Since a chunk is made of one Archetype, the arrays of components stored in it are all of the same length, so whatever entity index you access in one chunk, you will always get the same set of components.

Those chunks are great for CPU performance and are used injobs that implement`IJobChunk`

Checkthis quick youtube videowhich explains what chunks are.

## Queries

ECS - Entity Component System
ECS - Entity Component System
Editor: Asset and Prefab Inspector
text:
# Editor: Asset and Prefab Inspector

Source: https://cs2.paradoxwikis.com/Editor:_Asset_and_Prefab_Inspector

Verified by Paradox Interactive
This article has been verified by Paradox Interactive for version 1.5.2 f1 of the game.

## Prefab Component

The prefab component is always the top-most component shown in the inspector, and has a highlighted component header to differentiate from standard components.

The type of prefab component is dependent on the prefab preset you selecting duringImporting, and the different types affect how your asset can be used.

Common prefab component types:

- Building Prefab- used for main assets and also sub building upgrades.
- Building Extension Prefab- must be used for on-the-lot extension upgrades.
- Static Object- used for most props, sub objects, trees etc.
- Render Prefab- Prefab component for meshes

An alternative for setting the correct Prefab Component if you don’t use the prefab presets is to duplicate an existing asset or prefab you know has the correct Prefab Component.

The minimum information that can usually be assigned in the prefab component is:

#### Name

This is the internal name, otherwise known as the prefab name. Often these are simply mirroring the naming conventions used for importing assets, Best Practice is to keep these concise, descriptive and as unique as possible so it can’t be confused for another prefab.

title: Editor: Asset and Prefab Inspector
text:
n these are simply mirroring the naming conventions used for importing assets, Best Practice is to keep these concise, descriptive and as unique as possible so it can’t be confused for another prefab.

#### Localization

This is an expandable list, where you set the display texts for your asset if it is going to be visible in the in-game menu to players. Generally localization isn’t necessary for assets that don’t show in the UI.

If you choose to have display texts for your asset or prefab, the minimum required language to support is en-US, the English texts. If you leave these texts empty, the asset will show as a blank name or blank description in-game. This means descriptions aren’t always necessary, for example on a self-evident prop.

Add extra languages in the list and provide the texts for those languages and if a player is loading the interface with that language, the asset will automatically show the correct texts. The languages supported by the base game can be added to this list so you can fully localize your asset for maximum compatibility.

You don't have to provide all translations for every language. Note: It is recommended to keep a sheet with translations per name and description to keep things tidy for yourself.

#### Meshes

title: Editor: Asset and Prefab Inspector
text:
ility.

You don't have to provide all translations for every language. Note: It is recommended to keep a sheet with translations per name and description to keep things tidy for yourself.

#### Meshes

Usually present as an expandable list on asset prefabs, allowing you to assign a mesh to the asset. Meshes usually contain the single mesh of the asset you are editing. Depending on the asset type, you can move the mesh in three axes or rotate.

It is also the Prefab Component where other basic asset information is controlled, like:

#### Circular

A checkbox to make lot or object shapeCircular

#### Access Type

Determines how the building is accessible; by setting which sides of the lot it can connect to adjacent networks with. To understand which side is which, imagine standing on the street in front of the asset, facing toward the asset.

title: Editor: Asset and Prefab Inspector
text:
ccessible; by setting which sides of the lot it can connect to adjacent networks with. To understand which side is which, imagine standing on the street in front of the asset, facing toward the asset.

- Front: Building is only accessible from the front of the lot.
- LeftCorner: Building is accessible from the front and left side of the lot.
- RightCorner: Building is accessible from the front and right side of the lot.
- LeftandRightCorner: Building is accessible from the front, right and left side of the lot.
- LeftandBackCorner: Building is accessible from the front, left and back side of the lot.
- RightandBackCorner: Building is accessible from the front, right and back side of the lot.
- FrontandBack: Building is accessible from front and back only.
- All: Buildings can be accessed from all directions.
- OnRoad: for assets set to snap to the middle of a road, eg: similar to the PortContainer CranefromBridges and PortsDLC.
- OnRoadArea: for assets set to snap to the sidewalk, eg: similar toFerry Stop - QuayfromBridges and PortsDLC.
Note: For corner-growing spawnable buildings, you should only useLeftCornerandRightCornerrespectively for the spawning algorithm to correctly grow in on a corner. This means you need 1 left corner asset and 1 right corner asset.

#### Lot Dimensions

title: Editor: Asset and Prefab Inspector
text:
should only useLeftCornerandRightCornerrespectively for the spawning algorithm to correctly grow in on a corner. This means you need 1 left corner asset and 1 right corner asset.

#### Lot Dimensions

For each parameter ofLot WidthandLot Depthyou can enter the number of cells the asset should measure. Editing the cell dimension value, the lot area will be expanded equally from the center of the lot. This will not change the position coordinates of any mesh on the asset.

Alternatively, you can use the expand/shrink options. If you want to expand your lot towards one specific direction, you can click on the respective button. As a result an extra cell row or depth will be added, and the coordinates of any meshes on the lot will be updated respective of the direction the new cells are added in to maintain the mesh’s relative location. Other sub objects, sub nets, sub areas etc will also have their coordinates updated to retain their relative position as well, regardless of being parented to a mesh or not.

## Components

title: Editor: Asset and Prefab Inspector
text:
e location. Other sub objects, sub nets, sub areas etc will also have their coordinates updated to retain their relative position as well, regardless of being parented to a mesh or not.

## Components

Beneath the Prefab Component is the place where all other components are stacked in order they are added to the asset or prefab. You can add new components by clicking the ‘add component’ button at the bottom of the inspector, which opens up a list of available components that can be added to the prefab - this list can be easily searched by entering part of the component name in the top search bar. Note: While the components listed are filtered based on the type of Prefab Component you are inspecting, it is still possible to add a combination of components that may be incompatible with each other in certain situations. Always test if your asset works as expected and doesn’t cause any unexpected side effects, or crashes when interacted with.

### Component Operations

Most of the components for prefabs come with certain functionalities that can be useful for your editor workflow. These are Copy, Paste, Delete, Activate/Disable Components.

#### Copy & Paste

```
 Editor Component CopyPaste

```

title: Editor: Asset and Prefab Inspector
text:
fabs come with certain functionalities that can be useful for your editor workflow. These are Copy, Paste, Delete, Activate/Disable Components.

#### Copy & Paste

```
 Editor Component CopyPaste

```

Copy & Paste is a very useful tool allowing you to carry over data between components or across different assets. You can use this feature to copy paste color variations, props, surfaces, and networks to name a few examples.

The copy paste function only works if both assets or prefabs have two identical components.

For instance, if you wish to copy the color variations from one render prefab to another, you need to make sure the receiving render prefab has thecolor propertiescomponent added to it, to be able to paste onto. All you need to do then is click the ‘Copy’ icon on thecolor propertiescomponent on the source render prefab, then navigate to the receivingcolor propertiescomponent and click the ‘Paste’ icon to apply the copied data.

#### Delete

Editor Component Active

You can use the delete option to delete the entirety of the component. This is useful to mass delete all surface or sub objects without individually deleting them. You can add back deleted components later that are wiped of previous data, with some exceptions.

#### Activate/Disable

title: Editor: Asset and Prefab Inspector
text:
ul to mass delete all surface or sub objects without individually deleting them. You can add back deleted components later that are wiped of previous data, with some exceptions.

#### Activate/Disable

You can toggle the visibility of features of a component, without deleting them. Untick the box located on the top right side of the component. This is useful when you are working with complex assets and prevents accidental deletion of surfaces or networks.

## Object State Panel

Editor PreviewState2

Depending on the asset, you can preview how the asset can work in a live environment directly in the editor. Select the asset from asset browser, or click an already placed in editor world space. You will see the following options on the Object State Panel located on the lower right, where you can:

title: Editor: Asset and Prefab Inspector
text:
e editor. Select the asset from asset browser, or click an already placed in editor world space. You will see the following options on the Object State Panel located on the lower right, where you can:

- Simulate electricity connection to a building and check how it looks when it is operational.
- Adjust resident occupancy to see how the building looks if it’s completely empty or fully occupied. This is useful to check for parallax rooms and their render during night time.
- Preview color variations and check the asset would look like with different color variations.
- Preview animations and check how they appear depending on building efficiency.
- Trigger fire on the asset and preview how fire effects would function.
- Trigger collapse or accident on the asset and preview how smoke effects would function.
Note: Some of these options become available as relevant components or sub objects are added to the asset.

text:
# Editor: Interface

Source: https://cs2.paradoxwikis.com/Editor:_Interface

Verified by Paradox Interactive
This article has been verified by Paradox Interactive for version 1.5.2 f1 of the game.

## Asset Importer

Asset Importer is the tool for adding custom meshes and textures that can be used on custom assets. SeeAssets: Importing

## Asset Browser

Editor AssetBrowser Context

- Asset Browser is the repository that allows access to existing game prefabs and assets.
- The browser has multiple ways of sorting through the list.

- A search function. The search menu retains your previous searches.
 - Filters in a dropdown menu just below the search bar.
 - Categories located on the left panel that can be expanded and collapsed.

- Custom Assets category contains assets and prefabs you created and stored locally.
- Subscribed Assets category contains custom assets you are subscribed to from PDX Mods that are in your active playset. They will be listed with the asset name followed by its PDX Mods ID in brackets.
- The context menu for each listed asset can be accessed by clicking the (⋯) button, located on the right side of each asset name.
- The context menu provides additional options:

title: Editor: Interface
text:
Mods ID in brackets.
- The context menu for each listed asset can be accessed by clicking the (⋯) button, located on the right side of each asset name.
- The context menu provides additional options:

- Duplicate and edit - creates a local copy which you can then edit.
 - Duplicate and place - create a local copy that is then auto-selected to be placed in the world space. This copy can then be edited.
 - Edit - only your own custom, locally created asset and prefabs can be directly edited. Otherwise use the ‘Duplicate and edit’ option to create a local copy that you can then edit.
 - Place - places the selected asset.

- You can favorite prefabs you might use often, by hovering over the name and clicking the star icon. Favourited prefabs will bypass alphabetical listing and always show up first in the asset browser.
- You can also create your own custom prefabs by clicking the ‘Create New Prefab’ button.

## Photo Mode

Photo mode activates the identical tool that is available in-game to take screenshots with custom camera filters and settings.

## Bulldoze Mode

Bulldoze Mode allows you to delete objects from the editor world space. The mode allows the deletion of the whole asset, only sub elements (props, surfaces, lanes, nets, effects, markers) or everything - whether whole or sub element.

title: Editor: Interface
text:
e objects from the editor world space. The mode allows the deletion of the whole asset, only sub elements (props, surfaces, lanes, nets, effects, markers) or everything - whether whole or sub element.

You can also toggle underground mode to delete any asset or sub element located underground.

## Simulation Overrides

You can override time of the day, season, and cloudiness any time. These overrides are useful to simulate the look of the asset in different times of the day, season of the year or different weather conditions. Since Cities: Skylines II is a PBR based game, it is recommended to explore these settings and check if your assets and textures look as intended under different conditions. A good practice is to check your asset under clear weather and when it is overcast.

## Workspace

Editor Workspace Context

- It is your workdesk during your time in the editor.
- It can list assets that are ready (packaged) to be published on Paradox Mods. The list includes all assets that are packaged in the current session as well as previous.
SeeAssets: Package, Share and Upload

title: Editor: Interface
text:
n list assets that are ready (packaged) to be published on Paradox Mods. The list includes all assets that are packaged in the current session as well as previous.
SeeAssets: Package, Share and Upload

- It lists prefabs which can be edited by the player.
- Since editor world space is technically a map, workspace also features a ‘Map’ container by default.
- When you place any asset in the editor, they will be listed in a container called ‘Objects’ which will be nested in the Maps container.
- Objectscontainer will be created when you import custom objects or use the asset browser to place base game / custom assets.
- It functions similar to the scene outliner of a 3D modeling software, listing all placed objects in the editor world space by the asset creator.
- PrefabsLists custom prefabs created or duplicated by the player during the session. You can easily access them through here and do adjustments.
- Packageswill list any local assets you have packaged through the context menu. Packaged assets are ready to be published. For packaged assets, you can use the share option and upload them to Paradox Mods.
- Workspace context menu for each listed asset can be accessed by clicking the (⋯) button, located on the right side of each asset name.
- The context menu provides additional options:

title: Editor: Interface
text:
o Paradox Mods.
- Workspace context menu for each listed asset can be accessed by clicking the (⋯) button, located on the right side of each asset name.
- The context menu provides additional options:

- Save - saves the current asset or prefab locally.
 - Duplicate and edit - creates a local copy which you can then edit.
 - Duplicate and place - create a local copy that is then auto-selected to be placed in the world space. This copy can then be edited.
 - Package - packages selected items into a`.cok`file and processes compatible files for virtual texturing.
 - Share - allows you to upload your asset to Paradox Mods.

SeeAssets: Package, Share and Upload

- When you work with many assets in one session, the editor world space can have many objects placed. You can use the pin icon located next to the asset you want to work on. The camera centers the pinned asset.
- You can also directly remove placed assets by clicking the bulldoze icon next to the specific object.

## Infoviews

You can visualise the wind direction, pollution, resources, outside connections, transportation and electricity. Some of these options are only relevant and functional when you are creating a map.

## World Camera

You are provided with two camera options located in the top bar:

title: Editor: Interface
text:
, transportation and electricity. Some of these options are only relevant and functional when you are creating a map.

## World Camera

You are provided with two camera options located in the top bar:

- Orbit mode is the default camera mode.
- First Person mode allows you to zoom in much closer to the ground. This mode is useful when you are working with buildings. You can toggle between both modes whenever necessary.

## Advisor & Pause Menu

Advisor contains popup messages that explain most of the functions in the editor. You can open the Advisor by clicking its button and browse through the list for tips and information. Clicking any topic will start the sequence of popup infoboxes.

The Pause menu opens the menu to see the options button, exit button that returns you to the main menu and will show whether you are logged into Paradox Mods or not. The shortcut to open the pause menu is the Esc key.

## Inspector

The inspector will appear on the right of the screen in the editor whenever an asset is selected or editor tool. The current inspectors include:

title: Editor: Interface
text:
t to open the pause menu is the Esc key.

## Inspector

The inspector will appear on the right of the screen in the editor whenever an asset is selected or editor tool. The current inspectors include:

- Map Inspector - edit basic map settings and view the map checklist.
- Climate Inspector - view, edit and create custom climate prefabs that can be assigned to a map.
- Water Inspector - view, add and edit water sources and water settings.
- Resource Inspector - edit map resources or import resource location data.
- Terrain Inspector - use terrain tools, import heightmaps.
- Environment Inspector - view, edit and create custom water rendering settings or terrain rendering settings that can be assigned to a map.
See alsoMap Creation

- Asset and Prefab Inspector

- Object State Panel

See alsoEditor: Asset and Prefab Inspector

text:
# Editor: Snapping and Tool Modes

Source: https://cs2.paradoxwikis.com/Editor:_Snapping_and_Tool_Modes

Verified by Paradox Interactive
This article has been verified by Paradox Interactive for version 1.5.2 f1 of the game.

## Objects, Props, Decorations, Trees

### Tool Modes

For sub objects, the tool mode provides 4 options of placement:

- Place Individual(Label 1): - This is the default mode of sub object placement, allowing you to place one prop at a time. Place individual mode doesn’t have any unique snapping feature.
- Draw Line(Label 2),Draw Curve(Label 3): - Line modes allow the placement of props on either straight or curved lines, with a specific distance between prop instances.

- The distance(Label 9): - is a parameter that indicates the interval of length between prop instances placed along the line. The min/max distance is unique per prop, determined by the collision box of the prop mesh.
 - Snap to angle(Label 10): - Allows you to drag any line with a perpendicular angle.
 - Snap to distance(Label 11): - If enabled, the line tool will always respect the distance between props. If disabled, the line tool will dynamically adjust the distance with respect to the length of the entire line, giving you a bit more flexibility.

title: Editor: Snapping and Tool Modes
text:
e tool will always respect the distance between props. If disabled, the line tool will dynamically adjust the distance with respect to the length of the entire line, giving you a bit more flexibility.

- Place Multiple(Brush Mode) (Label 4): - Brush Mode allows you to paint areas with the object you selected. Brush mode is only available for map decorations and cannot be used on building/prop decoration.

- Brush Type(Label 12): - Set the stencil type of the brush from the list of available shapes in the base game.
 - Brush Size(Label 13): - Radius of the brush.
 - Brush Strength(Label 14): - Density of the brush (the number of instances you can place per stroke)
 - Brush Angle(Label 15): - Rotates the brush stencil.
 - Snapping options(Label 16): - for Brush Tool is more limited compared to the first 3 modes, with only upright mode being available. Brush tool is also a functional eraser tool, which can be used to mass delete props from an area. If you want to customize what to delete.
 - Delete selected type(Label 17): - If you have a prop selected and use eraser function, this option will allow you to mass delete only the selected prop from the area.

### Snapping Options

Editor Snapping01

The following snapping options are shared across the above tool modes. These are the most common snapping options you will be using for most of the propping operations:

title: Editor: Snapping and Tool Modes
text:
ing Options

Editor Snapping01

The following snapping options are shared across the above tool modes. These are the most common snapping options you will be using for most of the propping operations:

- Binds overlapping items to a building(Label 8): - This setting is the primary snapping option. It must be enabled to allow the placement of assets onto other assets for decoration purposes.
When this option is disabled, all placed objects will be treated as independent entities within the editor's world space, and will not be considered sub-objects.
- Snap to surface of the object(Label 6): - Allows the sub-object to be snapped to the geometry of the mesh. Useful for props such as wall lights and rooftop decorations. Placed sub-objects will retain their upright position if snap upright mode is enabled.
- Snap upright(Label 7): - Forces sub-object to retain their upright position at all times.
- Toggle all on/off(Label 5): - Quick switch of all snapping modes.

## Fences, Networks, Lanes, Areas and Surfaces

Fences, networks, lanes, areas and surfaces provide a variety of snapping options and additional tools. You should be familiar with some of these modes in-game. For use in-editor, there are some extra modes. Note: Network Tools support elevation - you can drag elevated networks or go underground.

### Tool Modes

title: Editor: Snapping and Tool Modes
section: Fences, Networks, Lanes, Areas and Surfaces
text:
d be familiar with some of these modes in-game. For use in-editor, there are some extra modes. Note: Network Tools support elevation - you can drag elevated networks or go underground.

### Tool Modes

- Straight Line(Label 1): - place a straight segment from point A to point B.
- Simple Curve(Label 2): - place a segment by defining a start, bend and then end point.
- Complex Curve(Label 3): - place a segment by defining the start, a middle point, 2 bends and an end point.
- Continuous(Label 4): - place a continuous series of segments that continuously bend.
- Grid(Label 5): Only available for networks. Define point A, then point B and drag sideways to form a grid.
- Replace(Label 6): - replace an already placed network or lane.
- Point(Label 7): - Allows you to place a single node of a net or a lane. These are used to create connection points for nets such as power lines and water pipes.
- Parallel(Label 8): - Allows you to use the same parallel tool that is available in-game.
- Elevation(Label 9): - Only available for networks. Change the elevation and elevation step for the network tool you have selected.

### Snapping Options

title: Editor: Snapping and Tool Modes
section: Fences, Networks, Lanes, Areas and Surfaces
text:
same parallel tool that is available in-game.
- Elevation(Label 9): - Only available for networks. Change the elevation and elevation step for the network tool you have selected.

### Snapping Options

- Snap to existing geometry(Label 10): - Allows snapping to already placed fences, networks, lanes, areas or surfaces.
- Snap to nearby geometry(Label 17): - Allows fences and lanes to snap to nearby fences and lanes.
- Snap to zoning cell length(Label 11): - Incrementally increases the distance of the dragged network by the length of a cell (8m), unique to networks.
- Snap 90 degree angles(Label 12): - Allows you to drag any line with a perpendicular angle.
- Snap to guide lines(Label 13): - Allows the alignment of nearby lanes and fences.
- Snap to zone grid(Label 14): - When networks that support zoning are placed in the editor’s world space, using this snapping option will allow you to snap to the cells of the zoning grid of these networks.
- Snap to surface of an object(Label 15): - Allows fence nodes to be snapped to a mesh. Useful for fences that are located on buildings.
- Snap to lot grid(Label 16): - Depending on the tool selected, the lot grid can be snapped to in different ways:

- For networks: Snaps the network tool to the centers of the cells that form the lot grid.
 - For areas, surfaces, & lanes: Snaps the tool to the edges of cells that form the lot grid.

section: Fences, Networks, Lanes, Areas and Surfaces

ifferent ways:

- For networks: Snaps the network tool to the centers of the cells that form the lot grid.
 - For areas, surfaces, & lanes: Snaps the tool to the edges of cells that form the lot grid.

Cities:Skylines Modding

Extra Assets Importer(EAI), is a code mod of Cities Skylines 2 that allow to import custom surfaces, decals and netlanes into the game. It is used by other game mods, usually in the form of theme packs released on the official Paradox Mods platform, but also in the form of local assets. Local assets can be created and modified by all tinkering users. Extra Assets Importeris particularly popular with players who want to detail their cities.

title: Extra assets importer/Basics
text:
# Extra assets importer/Basics

Source: https://cs2.paradoxwikis.com/Extra_assets_importer/Basics

Game modification:Extra assets importer

Ambox warning blue construction This article is undergoing a majorreconstruction.
You are welcome to assist in its overhaul.

## Installing Extra Assets Importer

Install with paradox mods or with Skyve.

#### Recommended mods

Dependencies: Extra Lib (Triton Supreme)

It is recommended to use these mods to take advantage of all the features of Extra Assets Importer:

- Anarchy (yenyang)Required. Allows to place assets without restrictions.
- Better Bulldozer (yenyang)Required. Allows you to delete assets.
- Extra Detailing Tools (Triton Supreme) Allows you to move decals with precision.
- MoveIt (Quboid/yenyang) Allows you to move decals and netlanes quickly.
- Area Bucket (Cmyna) Allows to fill, replace and preview surfaces.

## Adding Assets for Extra Assets Importer

Most assets are bundled into packs distributed as mods on the Paradox Mods platform. They can be installed like any other mod: directly in-game, via the Paradox Plaza website, or with the help of the Skyve tool. The description of these mods will usually specify that Extra Assets Importer is required for them to work properly.

There are also asset packs distributed in other forms, intended for more advanced users:Local asset packs

title: Extra assets importer/Basics
section: Adding Assets for Extra Assets Importer
text:
e mods will usually specify that Extra Assets Importer is required for them to work properly.

There are also asset packs distributed in other forms, intended for more advanced users:Local asset packs

These packs are not mods in the strict sense. They are usually provided as compressed archives (most often in .zip format) and distributed through community platforms such as Discord or GitHub.

For security reasons, it is strongly recommended to only download from reliable and identifiable sources. Ideally, make sure that:

- the files come directly from the author,
- the author is clearly identifiable,
- users have a free space to provide feedback or report issues (such as a forum or Discord server).

Only use links from YouTube or Reddit when they point to Paradox Mods. Do not use file-sharing platforms (e.g. X-Drive, X-Sharing, etc.) If in doubt, do not download the file. Also view themod securitypage.

Once the pack is downloaded, it must be extracted into the user directory as follows:%USERPROFILE%\AppData\LocalLow\Colossal Order\Cities Skylines II\ModsData\ExtraAssetsImporter\

While strictly following these rules:

- Surfaces must be placed in the /CustomSurfaces folder.
- Decals must be placed in the /CustomDecals folder.
- Netlanes must be placed in the /CustomNetlanes folder.

title: Extra assets importer/Basics
section: Adding Assets for Extra Assets Importer
text:
strictly following these rules:

- Surfaces must be placed in the /CustomSurfaces folder.
- Decals must be placed in the /CustomDecals folder.
- Netlanes must be placed in the /CustomNetlanes folder.

Each of the three main folders you just created contains subfolders, which act like categories — for example Roads, Ground, or Grass. Inside each category, every asset has its own folder. The folder’s name is the same as the asset’s name, and inside you will find:

- a .json file (sometimes two if it’s a netlane),
- one or more .png image files.

⚠️ It is very important to keep this exact folder structure. If a file is placed in the wrong folder, the game will not recognize the asset.

Since the latest updates of Extra Assets Importer, a new import system has been introduced. Assets that use this new system still follow the same folder rules, but they must be placed inside /Surfaces and /Decals instead of /CustomSurfaces and /CustomDecals.

Don’t worry: both systems work at the same time. Just check the author’s instructions carefully and put the files in the right place. Local mod

Once the local mod is downloaded, it must be extracted into the user directory as follows:%USERPROFILE%\AppData\LocalLow\Colossal Order\Cities Skylines II\Mods\

## Using Extra Assets Importer

Click on landscaping

Click on EAI-Surfaces Surfacesor EAI-Decals Decalsor EAI-Netlanes Netlanes

Click to select a subcategory, then choose the asset you want to use.

EAI Basics Surfaces1

All assets have predefined parameters set by their creator. These parameters determine the type of surfaces or objects the asset can be applied to (ground, roads, buildings, props, etc.), as well as the order in which multiple assets overlap (which ones appear above or below others).

Currently, these parameters are defined by the author in the asset’s JSON file and cannot be changed in-game. However, it is possible to create a local copy of the asset and edit its JSON file to adjust these settings.

title: Extra assets importer/json files
text:
# Extra assets importer/json files

Source: https://cs2.paradoxwikis.com/Extra_assets_importer/json_files

Game modification:Extra assets importer

Ambox warning blue construction This article is undergoing a majorreconstruction.
You are welcome to assist in its overhaul.

All information on this page concerns only the “new importers,” which are currently in beta and may be subject to change. Please refer to the “old importers” section for additional details.

## Overview

The JSON file controls surfaces, decals and netlanes behavior by defining their properties directly. It is advised to only include values that differ from default settings in JSON files. EAI will always set the value needed to make the asset work (default value).

## Material.json

#### _Metallic

default: 1
Values from 0 to 1.
0 is no metallic, 1 full metallic.
The _Metallic parameter is used to control the intensity of metallic effects. This will adjust the red channel from the _MaskMap.png if the file exists.

#### _Smoothness

default: 1
Values from 0 to 1.
0 is no smooth, 1 full smooth (will reflect light).
The _Smoothness parameter is used to control the intensity of shine effects. This will adjust the alpha channel from the _MaskMap.png if the file exists.

#### _colossal_DecalLayerMask

Default: 1
Terrain = 1
= 2
Buildings = 4
Vehicles = 8
Creatures = 16
Other (Props) = 32

title: Extra assets importer/json files
text:
ts. This will adjust the alpha channel from the _MaskMap.png if the file exists.

#### _colossal_DecalLayerMask

Default: 1
Terrain = 1
= 2
Buildings = 4
Vehicles = 8
Creatures = 16
Other (Props) = 32

This options will allow to control over which objects the surfaces, decals or netlanes will be rendered. Can be combined for example Terrain (1) + (2) = 3; Terrain + + Buildings + Vehicles + Other =47 and so.

#### _Draw Order

default: 0
Values from -170 to 200.
This value set the order in wich different assets (decals, surfacer or netlanes) are rendered.
Surfaces: -170 to 0. You can use bigger values but will be rendered under the decals.
Decals/Netlanes: 0 to 200. You can use negative values but will be rendered under the surfaces.

Considerations for different assets
Surfaces:Values for Ground and work fine, but have in mind that if value is set to Roads, the surface will bleed/overlap on elevated segments. Is not recommended to set any other option, like Buildings, Vehicles, Creatures or Other, because anything that be inside the surface are, will be overlapped by the surface texture.

title: Extra assets importer/json files
text:
elevated segments. Is not recommended to set any other option, like Buildings, Vehicles, Creatures or Other, because anything that be inside the surface are, will be overlapped by the surface texture.

Decals and Netlanes:Values of decals must be considered for the possible usage of them. If you set Ground, Roads, Buildings, Vehicles and Props (47) and also your decal is "thick" is quite probable they became hard to use, because will be rendered over all stuff, creating an ugly bleeding effect in near objects or surfaces. Do not use values for everything if your decal only make sense to use on Ground only, for example. If your decal is a logo, consider using Building, Vehicle and Other (Props), but avoid using and Terrain (Note: At this moment, rendering on Terrain is unavoidable for unknown reason, so if you use and Buildings only, decals will render on Terrain anyway.)

#### _AffectAlbedo

default: 1
0 or 1.
_AffectAlbedo controls whether your custom asset affects the colors of the underlying assets beneath it.

#### _AffectNormal

default: 1
0 or 1.
__AffectNormal controls whether your custom asset affects the normals of the underlying assets beneath it.

#### _AffectMetal

default: 1
0 or 1.
_AffectMetal controls whether your custom asset affects the metallic effect of the underlying assets beneath it.

#### _AffectSmoothness

#### _AffectMetal

default: 1
0 or 1.
_AffectMetal controls whether your custom asset affects the metallic effect of the underlying assets beneath it.

#### _AffectSmoothness

default: 1
0 or 1.
_AffectSmoothness controls whether your custom asset affects the smoothness of the underlying assets beneath it.

#### _MetallicAlphaSource

default: 0
0 or 1.
(To be tested)

#### _MetallicOpa city

default: 1
Values from 0 to 1.
0 creates translucency, allowing some visibility through to base layers, 1 results in complete opa city/solidity.
This parameter controls how visible the metallic effect (value or the _MaskMap red channel) is in relation to underlying surfaces.

MetallicOpa city=0 Metallic=1 Smoothness=1 (Translucent object)

MetallicOpa city=1 Metallic=0 Smoothness=1 (Mirror)

MetallicOpa city=0 Metallic=0 Smoothness=0 (Opaque)

#### _NormalAlphaSource

default: 0
0 or 1.
(To be tested)

#### _NormalOpa city

default: 1
Values from 0 to 1.
This parameter controls how visible the normal map is in relation to underlying surfaces: at value 0, the surface becomes transparent, revealing any base textures or materials underneath. At value 1, only the asset's own normal map remains fully visible

#### colossal_EdgeNormal

title: Extra assets importer/json files
text:
surfaces: at value 0, the surface becomes transparent, revealing any base textures or materials underneath. At value 1, only the asset's own normal map remains fully visible

#### colossal_EdgeNormal

Surfaces only.
default: 0.5
Values from 0 to 1.
This will adjust the bump effect at the edge of the surfaces. 0 is no bump, 1 is full bump.

#### colossal_UVScale

Surfaces only.
default: 0.2
Values from 2 to 0.00001. Not recommended to use values under 0.01
This will adjust the size of the mesh calculated as (1/side of the mesh in meters).

For example a 2x2 square will have 2 meter side mesh, (1/2)=0.5 The calculated UV Scale will be 0.5

#### _BaseColor

default: x: 1, y: 1, z: 1, w: 1
vector
The _BaseColor represents a solid color (tint) applied to the entire asset. This color in RGBA is multiplied by the _BaseColorMap.png and can be used to adjust color and transparency of the asset.

#### colossal_MeshSize

Decals and Netlanes only.
default: x: 1, y: 1, z: 1, w: 0
vector
This will adjust the size of a decal in meters. The size is defined as a 3D mesh dimension within the game environment. Regardless of your _BaseColorMap.png file's original dimensions, it will automatically scale or compress to fit these predetermined values while maintaining aspect ratio consistency.

The height (y-axis) value represents the decal thickness:

title: Extra assets importer/json files
text:
's original dimensions, it will automatically scale or compress to fit these predetermined values while maintaining aspect ratio consistency.

The height (y-axis) value represents the decal thickness:

- Thin decals may perform poorly on irregular surface geometries
- Thick decals risk bleeding into adjacent objects

Pixel Density:

- 1x1x1 size decal with a 1024x1024 pixels image will have a definition of ~1 pixel per millimeter.
- 2x1x2 size decal with a 1024x1024 pixels image will have a definition of ~half pixel per millimeter.
- 1x1x1 size decal with a 512x512 pixels image will have a definition of ~half pixel per millimeter.

Different decal types require varying image and mesh dimensions. For small decals (≤2 meters), high-resolution textures like 4K are unnecessary. Conversely, larger decals may benefit from higher resolutions but should maintain visual coherence with the game's overall aesthetic.

#### colossal_TextureArea

Decals and Netlanes only.
default: x: 0, y: 0, z: 1, w: 1
vector
This parameter adjusts the UV Map by specifying two corner points that establish a vector-based area. For optimal results, it's typically used in conjunction with adjustments to colossal_MeshSize. The coordinates are structured as X,Y for the bottom-left point and Z,W for the top-right point. The default settings use x:0,y:0 (bottom-left) and z:1,w:1 (top-right).

title: Extra assets importer/json files
text:
justments to colossal_MeshSize. The coordinates are structured as X,Y for the bottom-left point and Z,W for the top-right point. The default settings use x:0,y:0 (bottom-left) and z:1,w:1 (top-right).

```
"Vector": {
 "colossal_MeshSize": {
 "x": 1,
 "y": 1,
 "z": 1,
 "w": 0
 },

EAI-json TextureArea1

Texture Tiling:Creating Large Decals from Small Textures Texture tiling is the process of repeating a pattern across a surface. This technique allows artists to scale up small textures into larger decals without compromising visual quality or file size. In the following example, the texture is tiled to make a bigger decal. "MeshSize" is adjusted to prevent texture shrinking. To achieve correct enlargement of the decal, the example uses "colossal_MeshSize" x:2;z:2 and moves the top right point from coordinates z:1;w:1 to z:2;w:2, without moving x:0;y:0.

```
"Vector": {
 "colossal_MeshSize": {
 "x": 2,
 "y": 1,
 "z": 2,
 "w": 0
 },
 "colossal_TextureArea": {
 "x": 0,
 "y": 0,
 "z": 2,
 "w": 2
},

```

EAI-json TextureArea2

title: Extra assets importer/json files
text:
:2, without moving x:0;y:0.

```
"Vector": {
 "colossal_MeshSize": {
 "x": 2,
 "y": 1,
 "z": 2,
 "w": 0
 },
 "colossal_TextureArea": {
 "x": 0,
 "y": 0,
 "z": 2,
 "w": 2
},

```

EAI-json TextureArea2

Texture Crop:Texture crop refers to using only a part of the original texture by cropping between two points. It can be used with either one fixed point or two movable points to crop sections of the texture. The crop can be in a square or rectangular shape, depending on the points used. Using coordinates that are too close together or incorrectly positioning the points could trigger unexpected visual effects, such as squeezing, stretching, flipping, or rotation.

The following example demonstrates cropping different quadrants of the same texture. The white outlined quadrant uses coordinates x:0.5;y:0.5 and z:1;w:1 to crop the top-right quarter of the texture. To maintain proper scale, the size of "Texture_Mesh" must be adjusted accordingly—in this case, halved.

```
"Vector": {
 "colossal_MeshSize": {
 "x": 0.5,
 "y": 1,
 "z": 0.5,
 "w": 0
 },
 "colossal_TextureArea": {
 "x": 0.5,
 "y": 0.5,
 "z": 1,
 "w": 1
},

```
