## Prefab.json

Game. Prefabs. UIObject : Is the component in charge to place your prefab in the UI.

#### m_Group: Type

default: "Prefab Type"
"StaticObjectPrefab" for decals // "SurfacePrefab" for surfaces (to be checked!)
Controls UI category assignment, managed automatically by EAI when unset (recommended). Allows surface category customization independently of your mod's folder hierarchy.

#### m_Group: Name

default: "Prefab Name"
Controls UI name, managed automatically by EAI when unset.

#### m_Priority

default: -1
Values from 0 to 99999999.
Is the value that set which place in the in game menu the asset will be shown in it's own category. A value 0 will show it first of all, but if other assets also have a value of zero, will be shown in loading order.
Old importers: the name of this setting has changed and is UiPriority when using old importers

#### m_icon

default: null The m_icon serves as the path to your asset's icon. If left null, EAI will automatically handle the icon assignment (recommended). You can manually set this value if you need to customize the icon or share icons across multiple assets.

#### Game. Prefabs. ObsoleteIdentifiers

default:

```
"Game. Prefabs. ObsoleteIdentifiers": {
 "PrefabIdentifiers": [
 {
 "m_Name": "Prefab Name",
 "m_Type": "Prefab Type"
 }
 ]
}

```

title: Extra assets importer/json files
Extra assets importer/json files
text:
re sharing is a feature of the importer that allows multiple assets to reference the same texture, instead of duplicating it for each asset. This reduces both the pack size and its database footprint.

⚠️ path and CID are two different ways to do texture sharing, but they cannot be used at the same time.

#### How to use

To enable texture sharing, instead of providing a texture file (e.g. _BaseColorMap.png), you must replace it with a JSON file named the same but with the .json extension (e.g. _BaseColorMap.json).

Inside this JSON, you insert the template below depending on whether you want to use path or CID.

#### Using path

```
{
 "path": "Decals\\Graffiti\\CRIME"
}

```

- Works only within the same pack/mod.
- If the texture is not yet generated, it will be created automatically.
- The mod will then look for the texture in the following location:

Decals\Graffiti\CRIME\_BaseColorMap.png

- Best when you want to share textures inside a single pack.

#### Using CID

```
{
 "CID": "816357ca37d4b9f53a4aa36f2fc9c9b9"
}

```

- Searches the global database (all game assets).
- If the texture is not present in the database, nothing is found.
- Best when you want to reference textures that exist outside your pack.

## External links

title: Extra assets importer/Local assets
text:
# Extra assets importer/Local assets

Source: https://cs2.paradoxwikis.com/Extra_assets_importer/Local_assets

Game modification:Extra assets importer

Ambox warning blue construction This article is undergoing a majorreconstruction.
You are welcome to assist in its overhaul.

All information on this page concerns only the “new importers,” which are currently in beta and may be subject to change. Please refer to the “old importers” section for additional details.

## Overview

If you plan to release / share your work, you should turn your asset pack into a mod before sharing anything.

You can also directly create a mod and edit the file of your asset in your mod, that's surely the best way to do it. Why creating a mod is better? To avoid save break (white box everywhere in your save and in other people saves if you share it without being in a mod)

The Extra Assets Importer mod (EAI) introduces several custom asset importers that let you add new content to the game. The folder name determines which importer to use between old and new ones. Local assets exist in two forms:

- Placed directly in the folderc:\%USERPROFILE%\AppData\LocalLow\Colossal Order\Cities Skylines II\ModsData\ExtraAssetsImporter\
- Placed as a built mod withinc:\%USERPROFILE%\AppData\LocalLow\Colossal Order\Cities Skylines II\Mods\

The old importers (depreciated) include:

title: Extra assets importer/Local assets
text:
Order\Cities Skylines II\ModsData\ExtraAssetsImporter\
- Placed as a built mod withinc:\%USERPROFILE%\AppData\LocalLow\Colossal Order\Cities Skylines II\Mods\

The old importers (depreciated) include:

- Surfaces Importer – Import custom surfaces (e.g. grass, pavement). The files are placed in/CustomSurfaces/
- Decals Importer – Import custom decals (e.g. graffiti, stains). The files are placed in/CustomDecals/
- Net Lanes Decals Importer – Import decals that can curve (e.g. road markings). The files are placed in/CustomNetlanes/

The new importers (all in alpha) include:

- Surfaces Importer – Import custom surfaces (e.g. grass, pavement). The files are placed in/Surfaces/
- Decals Importer – Import custom decals (e.g. graffiti, stains). The files are placed in/Decals/
- Net Lanes Decals Importer – Import decals that can curve (curved decals, road markings). The files are placed in/NetLanesDecal/
- Localization Importer – Import localized text (translations) for your custom assets. The files are placed in/Localization/
- Asset Pack Importer – Define a custom asset pack as a group in the UI. Your mod’sroot foldercontains an AssetPack.json defining the pack. An optional SVG icon file (named after your mod) can be provided for the pack’s icon.

title: Extra assets importer/Local assets
text:
efine a custom asset pack as a group in the UI. Your mod’sroot foldercontains an AssetPack.json defining the pack. An optional SVG icon file (named after your mod) can be provided for the pack’s icon.

⚠️ Note: Custom assets are not officially supported by the base game yet. Breaking changes are possible as development continues. Each importer expects a specific folder structure and configuration files (in JSON format). This guide details the structure and JSON file formats for each importer, with examples. All importers are in alpha; their JSON schemas and functionality may expand or change in future versions.

## Folder & File Structure

EAI introduces several importer modules that parse specially‑structured folders and load custom assets into the game at runtime. The importers look for the following elements in each asset folder:

ElementFilesDescription

Base Color_BaseColorMap.pngbase color texture for the surface (diffuse/albedo)

Normal_NormalMap.pngadd shadow to the surface, to create a 3D effect

Mask_MaskMap.pnga combination of multiple elements to define material effects.

Iconicon.pngvisual representation of an asset in the in‑game user interface

PrefabPrefab.jsonholds the asset’s configuration parameters

MaterialMaterial.jsondefines the asset’s textures and material properties

title: Extra assets importer/Local assets
text:
gvisual representation of an asset in the in‑game user interface

PrefabPrefab.jsonholds the asset’s configuration parameters

MaterialMaterial.jsondefines the asset’s textures and material properties

The files must always be placed in folders following this structure to function: Asset type \ Category \ Asset name \

Make sure to strictly adhere to capitalization and lowercase letters.

Examples:
\Decals\Graffiti\ChickToGo StarQ 01\
\NetLanesDecal\RoadMarking\G87 UK Carriageway Line Red\
\Surfaces\Rock\Rzzirrah Old Cities Surfaces 1\

You will notice that these three examples follow precise rules for properly naming folders: first the author's name, followed by the asset name and potentially its number if it belongs to a series. Although this is not mandatory for it to function, these practices present numerous advantages such as easy searching with FindIt and prevent potential conflicts with other files.

A complete path example for your asset could be:

c:\%USERPROFILE%\AppData\LocalLow\Colossal Order\Cities Skylines II\ModsData\ExtraAssetsImporter\Decals\Graffiti\MyName MyBillboard 1\

Localization (optional) Localization is organized in a folder named Localization located at the root of the mod. Within this folder, each supported language has a JSON file—e.g., en-US.json for English or fr-FR.json for French. These files contain the translated strings used by the game.

title: Extra assets importer/Local assets
text:
at the root of the mod. Within this folder, each supported language has a JSON file—e.g., en-US.json for English or fr-FR.json for French. These files contain the translated strings used by the game.

Asset Pack (optional) Optionally, an SVG file bearing the same name as the mod (e.g., MyMod.svg) can be added to provide a custom icon that will appear in the game’s user interface. It is accompanied by an AssetPack.json file.

1 Source

ElementFilesDescription

LocalizationUS.json, fr-FR.jsonoptional text or translation for each language

Asset PackAssetPack.json + *.svgoptional icon to filter assets from the pack

## Image files

Image files for Cities: Skylines 2 must be in PNG format. The dimensions should each be a multiple of four, but square textures are recommended with side lengths that follow powers of two—such as 128×128, 256×256, 512×512, 1024×1024, 2048×2048, or 4096×4096.

PNG files can be created and edited using various graphics software includingGIMP,Photopeaor Photoshop. When working in Photoshop, it is highly recommended to utilize theSuperPNGplugin, as Photoshop does not natively support exporting PNG files with transparency on a separate alpha channel.

PNG files must use 8 bits per channel color depth. Compression is recommended, particularly usingSuperPNG's quantize compression method which optimizes file size without losing color data.

title: Extra assets importer/Local assets
text:
a channel.

PNG files must use 8 bits per channel color depth. Compression is recommended, particularly usingSuperPNG's quantize compression method which optimizes file size without losing color data.

Poorly optimized files can slow down downloads, use more disk space, and reduce game performance.

Note:Information from oversized files will not be used by the importer as they will be compressed during the import process.

#### _BaseColorMap

The_BaseColorMap.pngserves as the foundation for defining both color properties and transparency levels in your asset. The file contains RGB color channels that determine the visual appearance of your decal, while its alpha channel controls transparency levels across your asset's surfaces.

For optimal results when creating surface decals, it's recommended to test color settings in-game multiple times until the desired visual appearance. The game's built-in color management system requires specific adjustments to your textures for optimal appearance.

To manage colors effectively, you should desaturate and darken your image using tools like Photoshop's Hue/Saturation filter. A typical starting point is -25 points of saturation and lightness reduction, though this value can be adjusted based on personal preference.

Note: The game engine will automatically generate thumbnail icons from this file if no custom icon.png is provided.

title: Extra assets importer/Local assets
text:
ightness reduction, though this value can be adjusted based on personal preference.

Note: The game engine will automatically generate thumbnail icons from this file if no custom icon.png is provided.

#### _NormalMap

The_NormalMap.pngtexture adds depth to two-dimensional textures within CS2 by simulating surface details that would otherwise require complex 3D modeling. To create an effective normal map, you must use specialized software designed for generating these textures, such as Substance Sampler, NVIDIA Export Tools, orMaterialize. While some basic normal maps can be created using Photoshop, this approach requires a thorough technical understanding of the process and is best suited for users with advanced expertise in texture creation.

The game engine specifically implements theOpenGL standardformat for normal mapping.

While detailed technical explanations of how normal maps function in computer graphics are beyond this guide's scope, understanding these basic requirements is crucial for creating visually convincing city assets with enhanced surface details.

#### _MaskMap

The_MaskMaptexture utilizes channel separation for advanced effects on decals in Cities: Skylines 2.

The blue and green channels remain inactives and should be kept black, red and alpha channels serve distinct purposes:

title: Extra assets importer/Local assets
text:
tilizes channel separation for advanced effects on decals in Cities: Skylines 2.

The blue and green channels remain inactives and should be kept black, red and alpha channels serve distinct purposes:

- Red Channel: Governs metallic coating effects via the _Metallicity parameter. Values can range between 0 (completely non-metallic) and 1 (fully metallic), enabling customizable results based on desired visual outcomes.

The red channel is driven by the "_Metallic" value in json files.

- Alpha Channel: Controls the decal's shine or glossiness effect through the _Smoothness parameter in decal.json. This value ranges from 0 (no shine) to 1 (maximum intensity), with intermediate values allowing for precise adjustments.

The Alpha channel is driven by the "_Smoothness" value in json files.

A critical consideration is that without a corresponding _MaskMap.png file, both parameters must be set to zero in decal.json. Otherwise, the decal may exhibit mirror-like properties. However, for uniform application across entire decals without using _MaskMap, these parameters can still be effectively utilized.

#### Icon

title: Extra assets importer/Local assets
text:
n. Otherwise, the decal may exhibit mirror-like properties. However, for uniform application across entire decals without using _MaskMap, these parameters can still be effectively utilized.

#### Icon

Theicon.pngfile is the visual representation of an asset in the in-game user interface. It must adhere to strict dimension requirements of 128x128 pixels with the three RGB channels and an optional Alpha channel for transparency. While the game can automatically generate default icons, customizing them with descriptive names (such as "Leakage Large" or "Leakage Small") is highly recommended.

It is recommended to use the following model:

- In the top-right corner: your username or nickname in a short and easily recognizable form
- In the bottom-left corner: the descriptive name of the asset
- In the top-left corner: a visual aid symbol, to be used if the asset requires additional information

title: Extra assets importer/Surfaces
text:
# Extra assets importer/Surfaces

Source: https://cs2.paradoxwikis.com/Extra_assets_importer/Surfaces

Game modification:Extra assets importer

## To start

#### Step 1: Open menu

Click on landscaping

#### Step 2: Choose a category

Click on EAI-Surfaces Surfaces

#### Step 3: Select a subcategory

Click to select a subcategory, then choose the asset you want to use.

EAI-Surfaces-HowTo-1

#### Step 4: Draw your surface

Click on the ground with left mouse button to place points. Use right mouse button to delete points.

Important Note: The first two points define the surface orientation. For instance, if your surface is a tile, the tiles will stay aligned with these two points regardless of the position of subsequent points

EAI-Surfaces-HowTo-2

#### Step 5: Finish the area

Place your last point on the first point — tooltip will show "complete area".

EAI-Surfaces-HowTo-3

EAI-Surfaces-HowTo-4

## Quick tips for beginners

#### Use Anarchy mod

You can use Anarchy to remove placement limitations by enabling it through the button in the tool options panel.

For example, the common “Overlapping items” error that appears when placing surfaces can be disabled or ignored with Anarchy. This allows you to overlap surfaces and opens up new possibilities for creating interesting combinations.

#### Use Snapping options

title: Extra assets importer/Surfaces
text:
ears when placing surfaces can be disabled or ignored with Anarchy. This allows you to overlap surfaces and opens up new possibilities for creating interesting combinations.

#### Use Snapping options

Snapping options allows you to snap on existing surfaces, draw 90° angles and more.

EAI-Surfaces-Snapping

#### Delete surfaces

Use Better Bulldozer to delete surfaces. When dealing with multiple layers, it can sometimes be easier to use the snapping option (shown above): select a point and move it to create an invalid shape.

EAI-Surfaces-HowTo-8

title: How To Avoid Memory Leaks
text:
# How To Avoid Memory Leaks

Source: https://cs2.paradoxwikis.com/How_To_Avoid_Memory_Leaks

Please help with verifying or updating older sections of this article.
At least some were last verified forversion1.0.

Those who are new to modding Cities Skylines II may be unfamiliar with the concept of managed vs. unmanaged memory. ThisCommunity-Made Guideaims to help you understand what the difference between the two is, as well as what to do in order to avoid memory leaks.

## Managed vs Unmanaged Code

Both managed & unmanaged terms are related to what is known asgarbage collectionin programming. In most normal C# applications, thegarbage collectornormally handles themanagementof memory and makes sure that unused data is disposed of when no longer needed, freeing up memory for other tasks and ensuring that there are no memory leaks. However, Unity's Burst Compiler utilizesunmanagedmemory to help speed things up, as this automatic garbage collection can create significant computational overhead for massively parallel operations. When an automated garbage collector is present, we call this amanagedapplication, whereas an application without automatic garbage collection isunmanaged.

title: How To Avoid Memory Leaks
text:
l overhead for massively parallel operations. When an automated garbage collector is present, we call this amanagedapplication, whereas an application without automatic garbage collection isunmanaged.

Unity games that use DOTS are a bit of a hybrid system, because the Burst Compiler does not compile all code in the game's assembly, and much of it is still managed code. Game developers flag specific parts of the code to be burst compiled using the`[BurstCompile]`attribute. When you see this attribute attached to a struct/job in Unity, instances of that struct/job are going to be housed inunmanaged memory.

What this means is that developers need to manually allocate and deallocate memory for certain types of collections. You will need to do this for any Unity collections beginning with the wordsNativeorUnsafe. Burst compiled code will not accept managed collections such as C# Lists, etc, so you must use these unmanaged collections instead.

## Unity's Unmanaged Collections

Nativecollections are collections that contain safety checks to alert the developer that their allocations have persisted beyond the declared timeframe. Unsafecollections do not have these safety checks and may provide a slight performance boost, assuming that your code is written properly and you're certain that you're deallocating memory correctly.

title: How To Avoid Memory Leaks
text:
Unsafecollections do not have these safety checks and may provide a slight performance boost, assuming that your code is written properly and you're certain that you're deallocating memory correctly.

ForNativecollections, you are expected to declare the persistence of the allocation when creating the collection, measured in frames. There are three types of allocators built into Unity:`Temp`,`TempJob`,and`Persistent`. Generally speaking, allocations for shorter durations will spend less time allocating/deallocating the memory they need.

- `Temp`allocators exist for only one frame and are automatically disposed of at the end of the frame. These cannot be passed into a job, and are typically created within jobs to store collections that exist only within the scope of the job. These are the fastest types of memory allocations that offer the best performance.

- `TempJob`allocators persist for four frames, after which point it should be disposed of. There are a couple of frames of buffer time allowed before an error will be thrown to account for variances in processing time. Collections with this allocation type can be passed into a job. These are typically collections that are allocated during`OnUpdate`right before a job is scheduled.

title: How To Avoid Memory Leaks
How To Avoid Memory Leaks

title: Landscaping
text:
# Landscaping

Source: https://cs2.paradoxwikis.com/Landscaping

Please help with verifying or updating older sections of this article.
At least some were last verified forversion1.0.

## Terraforming

The Terraforming tab contains several tools that allow for changes in the terrain, which can help to change the physical design of the city. Soil is a resource which is accumulated by reducing the height of land, and is used elsewhere to increase land height. This enables the construction of new coastlines or mountain ranges or perhaps the removal of an inconvenient mountain. It can sometimes be helpful to level terrain so buildings look better, and even be necessary for larger buildings like airports. The tab contains the following tools:

- Shift Terrain - Elevate by pressing the primary mouse button, lower with the secondary mouse button.
- Level Flatten Terrain - Use the secondary mouse button to select the target height, then use the primary button to flatten terrain to that height.
- Soften Terrain - Use the primary mouse button for a gentle smoothing effect.
- Slope Terrain - Click with the primary mouse button on one end of the area where the slope is to be located. With the secondary mouse button, click and drag from the other desired end back towards the first point to create the slope.

title: Landscaping
text:
imary mouse button on one end of the area where the slope is to be located. With the secondary mouse button, click and drag from the other desired end back towards the first point to create the slope.

When terraforming, the size of the brush can be changed from 10 (equivalent to 7/8 zoning cells) to 1000. The strength of the brush can range from 1 to 100 percent.

## Vegetation

Similar to zoning, vegetation can have European or North Americanthemeto determine its appearance. Each piece of vegetation can be placed either one at a time at the cost of ₡10 apiece, or in bulk by using a brush similar to the terraforming one. Vegetation grows bigger as time passes.

- Sylvester Palm Tree

## Pathways

Pathways are narrow tracks which citizens will use in combination with roadside sidewalks and crosswalks to reach destinations on foot.

Paths are placed using the same tools asroads. They can be elevated, allowing players to construct footbridges over major roads as well as other, more elaborate pedestrian systems. Cut, fill, and tunnel paths can also be built.

There are three special pedestrian path bridge designs that can be built in addition to generic elevated paths. The designs are a cable-stayed bridge, a tied-arch bridge, and a modern covered bridge.

Citizens are prepared to walk surprisingly long distances to their destinations, and an investment in footpath infrastructure can relieve a considerable burden from a city's roads. Citizens will also walk parts of journeys that also involve other transport methods. If a road dead-ends and there is another road nearby, it can be helpful to link the dead-end to the other road with a pedestrian path to allow citizens on foot an easy way out.

### Pedestrian bridges

Arch Bridge

Covered Bridge

Cable-stayed Bridge

Cable-Stayed Pedestrian Drawbridge

title: Launch Parameters
text:
# Launch Parameters

Source: https://cs2.paradoxwikis.com/Launch_Parameters

## Available Parameters

ParameterDescription

`--disableModding`Disables all mod loading. Useful for troubleshooting issues caused by mods.

`--disableCodeModding`Disables code mods only (mods that inject/alter game code). Other types of mods/plugins may still load.

`--developerMode`Launches the game in developer mode, allowing the debug menu to be opened ingame using TAB key

`--uiDeveloperMode`Lauches the game in ui developer mode, allowing to access localhost:9444 for UI inspection and debugging

`--burst-disable-compilation`Disables burst compilation. Not recommended for normal gameplay as it slows the game down massively, but useful for debugging native crashes.

## How to Use Launch Parameters

### Via Steam

1. Open theSteamclient.
2. Go to yourLibrary.
3. Right-clickCities: Skylines II→ selectProperties.
4. Under theGeneraltab, find the fieldLaunch Options.
5. Enter the desired parameter(s), for example:

```
--disableModding
--disableCodeModding

```

You can combine multiple parameters by separating them with spaces:

```
--disableModding --disableCodeModding

```

1. Close the Properties window. Next time you launch the game via Steam, it will use those parameters.

### Via Microsoft Store / Xbox App

title: Launch Parameters
text:
with spaces:

```
--disableModding --disableCodeModding

```

1. Close the Properties window. Next time you launch the game via Steam, it will use those parameters.

### Via Microsoft Store / Xbox App

If you have the Microsoft Store / Xbox App version ofCities: Skylines II, you can’t set launch options via a built-in field, but youcanuse a custom shortcut to pass parameters to the executable.

1. Locate the game’s installation directory. Typically something like:

```
C:\XboxGames\Cities Skylines II\Content\

```

or under

```
C:\Program Files\ModifiableWindowsApps\...

```

depending on how the game was installed.

1. Find the game executable (`Cities2.exe`) in that folder.
2. Create a shortcut to that`.exe`.

- Right-click the`.exe`→Send to→Desktop (create shortcut)
 - Or right-click →Create shortcutin the same folder and move it somewhere you prefer.

3. Right-click the shortcut → selectProperties.
4. In theTargetfield, after the path to the`.exe`, add the desired launch parameter(s). For example:

```
"C:\XboxGames\Cities Skylines II\Content\Cities2.exe" --disableModding

```

Or to add more than one parameter:

```
"C:\XboxGames\Cities Skylines II\Content\Cities2.exe" --disableModding --disableCodeModding

```

title: Launch Parameters
text:
\Cities Skylines II\Content\Cities2.exe" --disableModding

```

Or to add more than one parameter:

```
"C:\XboxGames\Cities Skylines II\Content\Cities2.exe" --disableModding --disableCodeModding

```

1. (Optional) Rename the shortcut to indicate which parameters are active (e.g. Cities2 – NoMods).
2. Use this shortcut to start the game whenever you want those parameters applied.

## Notes

- When using Microsoft Store / Xbox App versions, sometimes file permissions or the way the store handles updates/modifications may interfere with custom shortcuts / exec arguments.
- Some anti-cheat or launcher wrappers may ignore or strip certain parameters.
- Always test after adding parameters to make sure the game still runs and the desired effect takes place.

title: Localize your mod
text:
# Localize your mod

Source: https://cs2.paradoxwikis.com/Localize_your_mod

ThisCommunity-Made Guidewill cover mod localization so you can make your mod available in multiple languages.

Even if you don't want to handle translations yourself, using translation keys allows other localization mods to add languages for your mod, so you are advised to avoid hardcoding any user-facing string.

## Setup translations files

### Standalone approach

Ambox content Undocumented yet, you're welcome to contribute! In the meantime, the Vanilla mod template already provides an example with its`LocaleEN`class.

### UsingI18n EveryWheredependency

I18n EveryWherecan help developers by preventing them to have to create their own localization system (ex. parsing JSON or CSV files, hydrating the game's locales manager, etc).

It can be used in two ways:

title: Localize your mod
text:
veryWherecan help developers by preventing them to have to create their own localization system (ex. parsing JSON or CSV files, hydrating the game's locales manager, etc).

It can be used in two ways:

- You provide an english translation in a traditional way but want to easily add new languages via JSON files. Pros: This makes your mod resilient toI18n EveryWherenot being loaded, or you can even skip adding it to your dependencies and tell users to install it if they want localization. Cons: Users have to install it separately, and you have to implement your own default localization.
- Completely skip implementing anything related to localization in your source code, and let the mod handle that for you, using a hard dependency. Pros: Much more simple and straightforward. Cons: You are now depending on another mod, and as such, third-party code.

In the case you want to use it:

- Create a new`lang`(lowercase) folder in your mod source folder.
- Add localization json, like`en-US.json`,`zh-HANS.json`, etc.
- Add this configuration to your project's`.csproj`file:

```
<ItemGroup>
 <Content Include="lang\**">
 <CopyToOutputDirectory>PreserveNewest</CopyToOutputDirectory>
 </Content>
</ItemGroup>

title: Localize your mod
text:
on`, etc.
- Add this configuration to your project's`.csproj`file:

```
<ItemGroup>
 <Content Include="lang\**">
 <CopyToOutputDirectory>PreserveNewest</CopyToOutputDirectory>
 </Content>
</ItemGroup>

```
This might also be done automatically or by clicking things in your IDE when you create the files, but in any case, you just have to ensure that when building your project, the`lang`folder is copied to the output directory, i.e.`C:\Users\You\AppData\LocalLow\Colossal Order\Cities Skylines II\Mods\YourMod\lang`.
- If you don't have a standalone default locale, add the mod as a hard dependency in`Properties/PublishConfiguration.xml`:

```
<Publish>
	<Dependency Id="75426" DisplayName="I18n EveryWhere" />
</Publish>

```

- That's really it!

## Translating strings, localizing dates and numbers

### In UI code (preferred)

It is best to translate and localize things (like dates, numbers, etc.) in your UI code, for two reasons:

- It is much easier as the game's .NET libraries do not provide any utility method to do that,
- You benefit from live refresh of the locale when the user changes it without needing to handle that yourself.

#### Translating strings

The simplest way to translate a string is to use the`translate()`function:

```
import { useLocalization } from 'cs2/l10n';

title: Localize your mod
section: Translating strings, localizing dates and numbers

title: Localize your mod
section: Translating strings, localizing dates and numbers
text:
ousands separator or translated and converted units, is a nice touch you can add to your mod. You can use this component:

```
import { LocalizedNumber, Unit } from 'cs2/l10n';

// ... in a component:

<LocalizedNumber
 unit={Unit. Temperature} // optional
 signed={true} // optional, if you want the number to have "+" or "-" suffix, 0 is "±".
 value={30} // you don't have to make °C/°F conversion! Read below.
/>

```

There's a bunch of units to choose from, let your IDE autocomplete list them for you!

Another practical thing when using an unit is that the game performs conversion from theISUunits if the user uses others, ex. 30 (celsius) with`Unit. Temperature`prints "86°F", or 100 (meters) with`Unit. Length`prints "109 yd".

Note that the output is rounded too, ex. 100 meters prints out 109 yards and not 109.361 yd.

#### Format fractions (with or without units)

To display fractions, you can use this component:

```
import { LocalizedFraction, Unit } from 'cs2/l10n';

// ... in a component:

<LocalizedFraction
 unit={Unit. Power} // optional
 value={50} // numerator
 total={100} // denominator
/>

```

The example above will print "5 / 10 kW".

#### Format bounds

To display a range between two min-max values, you may use this component:

```
import { LocalizedString, Unit } from 'cs2/l10n';

// ... in a component:

title: Localize your mod
section: Translating strings, localizing dates and numbers

title: Localize your mod
section: Translating strings, localizing dates and numbers
text:
n example for that, it's not pretty but it's simple (Cohtml's JS engine was stripped of standardIntl APIsthat would allow to do that in a one-liner):

```
const date = new Date('2024-01-01T13:00:00');

const timeStr = unitSettings.timeFormat == (0 satisfies TimeFormat. TwentyFourHours)
 ? date.getHours().toString().padStart(2, '0') +
 ':' +
 date.getMinutes().toString().padStart(2, '0')
 : (date.getHours() == 12 ? 12 : (date.getHours() % 12)).toString().padStart(2, '0') +
 ':' +
 date.getMinutes().toString().padStart(2, '0') +
 ' ' +
 (date.getHours() > 12 ? 'PM' : 'AM');

```

This will output "13:00" in 24-hours format and "01:00 PM" otherwise.

#### Format duration

You can format an approximate duration in months or years with this component:

```
import { LocalizedDuration } from 'cs2/l10n';

// ... in a component:

<LocalizedDuration
 value={3} // value in days
 daysPerYear={12} // mandatory, and remember that days are months are the same thing in CS, so there are 12 days per year.
 maxMonths={6} // optional, threshold where to stop displaying the date in months and use years instead
/>

```

This component won't get any more precise than "months" or "days", and when using years, it will round the value to the closest integer. For example, a`value=18`with`daysPerYear=12`will print "2 years".

text:
mods.

The structure of a custom keyshouldbe:`MyModNamespace[.SubNamespaces. OrFolders][.ClassOrReactComponent].YOUR_KEY_NAME_OR_TYPE[[VariantName]]`

Some examples for an hypothetical "Disaster" mod:

- `Disasters. Common. DISASTER_NAME[NukeCity]`: A key for a disaster name in a virtual "Common" namespace for reusing keys in different places.
- `Disasters. Common. DISASTER_NAME[AlienInvasion]`: Another disaster name.
- For an hypothetical React component in`UI/disaster-control/disaster-panel.tsx`:

- `Disasters. UI.DisasterControl. DisasterPanel. DISASTER_DIALOG_MESSAGE`: A key for a confirmation dialog message.
 - `Disasters. UI.DisasterControl. DisasterPanel. DISASTER_DIALOG_ACTIONS[StartDisaster]`: Key for a "Start Disaster" button in the aforementioned dialog.
 - `Disasters. UI.DisasterControl. DisasterPanel. DISASTER_DIALOG_ACTIONS[StaySafe]`: Key for a cancel button in the aforementioned dialog.

- Integrating into Vanilla keys:

title: Localize your mod
text:
the aforementioned dialog.
 - `Disasters. UI.DisasterControl. DisasterPanel. DISASTER_DIALOG_ACTIONS[StaySafe]`: Key for a cancel button in the aforementioned dialog.

- Integrating into Vanilla keys:

- `Options. OPTION[Disasters. Setting. DefaultDisasterStrength]`: For Options UI translations, youmustprovide a correctly-named key to Vanilla!
 - `ToolOptions. TOOLTIP_TITLE[Disasters. DisasterStrength]`: Example of a custom disaster tool option tooltip in a tool panel.
 - `ToolOptions. TOOLTIP_DESCRIPTION[Disasters. DisasterStrength]`: Using the typical`_DESCRIPTION`suffix.
 - `Tools. INFO[Disasters. StartDisaster]`: For a tool's tooltip under the cursor before launching the action.

### Vanilla translation keys & namespaces

Here's a table of Vanilla string categories as of build 1.1.2f1. This is not an exhaustive list, but it lists the most important and useful categories for modders, to:

- Reuse an existing translation,
- Add modded translation keys into existing categories,
- Understand how Vanilla structures its translations keys.

It is highly encouraged for modders to respect Vanilla keys (adding their namespace, eventually) and reuse them when possible, for example, if you're:

title: Localize your mod
text:
nd how Vanilla structures its translations keys.

It is highly encouraged for modders to respect Vanilla keys (adding their namespace, eventually) and reuse them when possible, for example, if you're:

- Wanting to localize a generic action/option name (ex. "Delete", etc),
- Add translations for features integrating in Vanilla's UI and systems (ex. a custom cursor tool, an asset name, etc),
- Wanting to format a parametrized string (variable interpolation) the same way Vanilla does, if there is not alreadyan UI helperfor that.

There are probably already keys to do that for all vanilla-supported languages, avoidingyou and your translatorsmore work!

Note that:

- `DESCRIPTION`keys aren't generally mandatory, if ever. However, please fill them in your mods when possible!
- The case, usage of spaces (or lack of, it can be inconsistent), the different kind of separators and suffix digits are representative of how keys are named.

Unfortunately, translations are encoded as binary files in the game installation folder and therefore cannot be read directly, so you can find a complete JSON export of Vanilla keys and values created against build 1.1.2f1 and sorted by key here: [REMOVED LINK, AWAITING REUPLOAD]. `StatisticsPanel.*`Statistics panel translations.

`Toolbar. SOME_ACTION_OR_TOOL`Toolbar main action translations and some tool options that probably should be in`ToolOptions. TOOLTIP*`.

`ToolOptions. TOOLTIP[OptionName]`

`ToolOptions. TOOLTIP_TITLE[OptionName]`

`ToolOptions. TOOLTIP_DESCRIPTION[OptionName]`Tool option title and description, e.g. "Complex Curve", "Snap to lot grid", etc. It isn't really about tooltips, but more about the little static dropdown that shows with a specific tool.

`Tools. SOME_USEFUL_REMARK`An info shown when using a tool or hovering an object, that helps you to place it or informs on its status, typically shows as a tooltip under your cursor, e.g. "Groundwater Volume", "Flow", "Construction Cost", etc.

`Tools. INFO[ActionName]`Action that will be performed by the tool in use, e.g. "Bulldoze object", "Complete area", "Delete route", etc.

title: Localize your mod
text:
, e.g. "Groundwater Volume", "Flow", "Construction Cost", etc.

`Tools. INFO[ActionName]`Action that will be performed by the tool in use, e.g. "Bulldoze object", "Complete area", "Delete route", etc.

`Tools. TOOL_MODE[ModeName]`Mode of action for a tool, e.g. "Complex Curve", "Continuous", "Fill", "Paint", etc.

`Tools. WARNING[WarningName]`A warning that shows when a tool/building is improperly used, e.g. "Water level too low".

## Bonus

### Finding translators

You can setup a Crowdin project (or other translations sites) to help translators quickly update your mod's translation. You'll probably have much more success this way than by demanding people to fork and translate your Git repository.

If you decide to use Crowdin, please advertise it on your Paradox Mods web page by putting a link to your project using this configuration in your`Properties/PublishConfiguration.xml`file:

```
<ExternalLink Type="crowdin" Url="https://crowdin.com/project/your-project-name" />

```

Using this technique or by putting a link anywhere in the description of your mod, it will also be referenced in this list on GitHub:CitiesSkylinesModding/PdxModsIntlCrawler/List of Mods Seeking Translators.md.

You can also proactively find translators on theCities: Skylines Modding Discordcommunity, typically by posting a dedicated thread in`#mods-wip`.

title: Localize your mod
text:
xModsIntlCrawler/List of Mods Seeking Translators.md.

You can also proactively find translators on theCities: Skylines Modding Discordcommunity, typically by posting a dedicated thread in`#mods-wip`.

### Dump all keys and values from the locale dictionary

If you have installed I18N Everywhere, you can just select mods/assets in I18N Option tabs and click Export. Then you can get one or more files in mod's data directory.

Also, you may use this C# snippet to dump all available keys and values to a pretty-printed JSON file, here`C:\Users\You\AppData\LocalLow\Colossal Order\Cities Skylines II\locale-dictionary.json`(auto-import symbols as needed):

```
var strings = GameManager.instance.localizationManager.activeDictionary.entries
 .OrderBy(kv => kv. Key)
 .ToDictionary(kv => kv. Key, kv => kv. Value);

var json = Colossal. Json. JSON.Dump(strings);

var filePath = Path. Combine(Application.persistentDataPath, "locale-dictionary.json");

File. WriteAllText(filePath, json);

```

If you want to include translations from mods too, this code shoud run some time after your mod's`OnLoad()`, otherwise at this time only Vanilla keys are loaded.

title: Logging
text:
# Logging

Source: https://cs2.paradoxwikis.com/Logging

Please help with verifying or updating older sections of this article.
At least some were last verified forversion1.0.

## Creating a Log File

To create a logger, simply use`LogManager. GetLogger(fileName)`method from`Colossal. Logging`
This will create a log file for your mod in`%AppData%\..\LocalLow\Colossal Order\Cities Skylines II\Logs`

```
namespace MyCustomMod
{
 public class MyMod : IMod
 {
 public static ILog Log { get; } = LogManager. GetLogger(nameof(MyMod)).SetShowsErrorsInUI(false);

public void OnLoad(UpdateSystem updateSystem)
 {
#if VERBOSE
 Log.effectivenessLevel = Level. Verbose;
#elif DEBUG
 Log.effectivenessLevel = Level. Debug;
#endif

Log. Info("Loading..");

// Do Things
 }
 }
}

```

Make sure that your log's name matches your ModsSettings folder name and your Settings file name mentioned inthe naming guide.

## Changing the Log's Effectiveness

An`ILog`'s log level can be manually set. In the previous example, we set the level toVerboseorDebugbased on which symbol is specified in the mod's project.

By default, the log level is specified by the game, and can be globally changed using the launch parameter:

```
--logsEffectiveness=DEBUG

```

#### Available Log Levels

- DISABLED
- EMERGENCY
- FATAL
- CRITICAL
- ERROR
- WARN
- INFO
- DEBUG
- TRACE
- VERBOSE
- ALL

## Guidelines & Tips

title: Map Creation
text:
# Map Creation

Source: https://cs2.paradoxwikis.com/Map_Creation

Verified by Paradox Interactive
This article has been verified by Paradox Interactive for version 1.1.0 f1 of the game.

## The Basics of Creating a Map

### Map Panel

Selecting Map in the Workspace panel opens up the Map panel on the right of the screen. See also:Workspace Panel

#### Map Settings

Map Name
&
Map DescriptionChoose a language to support from the dropdown, by default there must be at least one name entered for English (en-US).

You can enter your chosen map name in the text field showing "Map name" next to the dropdown. Pressing the "add translation" button allows you to add a new translation for a different language.

Use Map name as default city nameTick this checkbox to use the Map Name as the first city name suggestion that is shown to the player in the new game panel. The player can still override this with their own city name in map options when starting a new game.

Starting Year, Month and TimeTick the"Use current year as starting year"checkbox to set the start year as the current calendar year. Uncheck to specify a different year.

Set the month the map will start a new game in. With 1 being January and 12 being December Set the starting time your map will start a new game with.

Camera Starting PositionChoose the position the camera will start with when your map is used in a new game.

title: Map Creation
section: The Basics of Creating a Map
text:
ng January and 12 being December Set the starting time your map will start a new game with.

Camera Starting PositionChoose the position the camera will start with when your map is used in a new game.

Using the "capture camera position" button will take a snapshot of the current view's Pivot coordinates, angle and zoom. You can adjust each of these parameters manually as well.

Select Starting TilesYour map will automatically generate the default layout for map tiles, including the standard 9 starting tiles in the center of the map. This button allows you to designate which map tiles will be unlocked when your map starts in a new game.

ThemeChoose the default theme your map shows in the New Game Panel. The player can still choose to change this in map options when they start a new game.

PreviewAllows you to select a screenshot to be used as the preview image for your map. This shows up on the right of the "New Game" panel.

ThumbnailAllows you to select an image to be used as the thumbnail icon for your map. This shows up in the "New Game" panel. Best practice is to use a top down view of your map.

AdvisorNotifications

Note

Preview and Thumbnail Images would typically be located inside`C://Users/[username]/AppData/LocalLow/Colossal Order/Cities Skylines II/Screenshots/`

title: Map Creation
section: The Basics of Creating a Map
text:
down view of your map.

AdvisorNotifications

Note

Preview and Thumbnail Images would typically be located inside`C://Users/[username]/AppData/LocalLow/Colossal Order/Cities Skylines II/Screenshots/`

- Preview Images should be 16:9 1920x1080 png files, no more than 1mb in filesize and the filename can contain no spaces, only alphanumerical characters, hyphens and underscore.
- Thumbnail Images should be 1:1 png files, no larger than 1024x1024, no more than 1mb in filesize and the filename can contain no spaces, only alphanumerical characters, hyphens and underscore.

#### Map Checklist

Required

Select starting tilesYou must have at least one tile selected as a start tile to have a functioning map.

Have surface or ground water in the starting areaIn order for a city to be able to have a successful start you need to provide a form of water resource in one or more of the starting tiles.

Have an outside road connectionYou map must have at least one inbound and outbound road connection. While not required, best practice would be to have your road connection enter at least one of the starting tiles.

Give your map a nameYour map must have at least an English (en-US) name before you can save.

Optional

title: Map Creation
section: The Basics of Creating a Map
text:
d, best practice would be to have your road connection enter at least one of the starting tiles.

Give your map a nameYour map must have at least an English (en-US) name before you can save.

Optional

- Outside train connection
- Outside airplane connection
- Outside electricty connection
- HaveResourcesin your map
While these are optional, it is recommended to provide players of your map with as much chance of success. However, there is still challenge for a map that is missing some or all of these items.

### Edit Climate Panel

AdvisorNotifications

Warning

Changes in the Climate panel, including editing Climate settings or editing seasons do not save in 1.0.21f1. This will be updated in the future.

Selecting Climate in the Workspace panel opens up the Climate Settings on the right of the screen. See also:Workspace Panel

#### Climate Settings

Load climate prefab...Select one of the built-in climate prefabs to load into climate settings as a template to edit.

Latitude
&
LongitudeSet the real-world location of your map to set the hemisphere for your map. This sets your sun positon.

Freezing TemperatureMeasured in Celsius - set to 0 by default. Changing this value will change when precipitation will turn from rain into snow. Best Practice for climates where snow does not occur is to set this value to lower than the lowest temperature on the map inSeasons.

title: Map Creation
section: The Basics of Creating a Map
text:
value will change when precipitation will turn from rain into snow. Best Practice for climates where snow does not occur is to set this value to lower than the lowest temperature on the map inSeasons.

DefaultWeatherLoadedDefaultContinentalby default. Use theLoad climate prefab..button to load in a new DefaultWeather prefab from a template.

Note:Prefab should not be changed using the drop down selector.

DefaultWeathersSix standard weather prefabs loaded.

Note:These prefabs should not be changed.

SeasonsFour standard seasons loaded: Winter, Spring, Summer & Autumn. Seasonscan be added, removed and edited here.

#### Seasons

PrefabSelect one of the four built-in season prefabs that best match the season you wish to create.

Name IDSet the name of your season.

Icon PathNot in use.

Start TimeAssign when the season starts during the year. Values range from 0 - 1. (0 - 1 = 12 months range)

Example:For the northern hemisphere, Winter starts at 0.917 and last until Spring starts at 0.167 (from beginning of December till the end of February) These values are fully customizable.

Temp Night DaySet the minimum and maximum temperature (in celsius) where x = lowest temperature and y = highest temperature. These values act as a center to temperature changes as the seasons change.

title: Map Creation
section: The Basics of Creating a Map
text:
Temp Night DaySet the minimum and maximum temperature (in celsius) where x = lowest temperature and y = highest temperature. These values act as a center to temperature changes as the seasons change.

Temp Deviation Night DaySet the maximum variation in temperature (in celsius) where x = maximum deviation from the lowest temperature and y = maximum deviation from the highest temperature. These values give your seasons a randomness that simulates the way seasons behave.

Cloud ChanceA percentage value (out of 100) of the chance there will be clouds in the sky.

Cloud AmountA percentage value (out of 100) of the amount of clouds in the sky. 100 = thick, dark clouds. 10 = very thin, sparse clouds.

Cloud Amount DeviationA percentage value (out of 100) of the deviation in the set amount of clouds in the sky. 100 = full range of deviation, less bespoke. 0 = no deviation, the game will only select the exact value of the amount of clouds fromCloud Amount.

Precipitation ChanceA percentage value (out of 100) of the chance there will be rain in the sky. Rain requiresclouds chanceto be satisfied.

Precipitation AmountA percentage value (out of 100) of the amount of rain. 100 = Heavy rain. 10 = very light rain. Rain is dependent on the chance and amount of clouds in the sky.

title: Map Creation
section: The Basics of Creating a Map
text:
nceto be satisfied.

Precipitation AmountA percentage value (out of 100) of the amount of rain. 100 = Heavy rain. 10 = very light rain. Rain is dependent on the chance and amount of clouds in the sky.

Precipitation Amount DeviationA percentage value (out of 100) of the deviation in the set amount of rainfall. 100 = full range of deviation, less bespoke. 0 = no deviation, the game will only select the exact value of the amount of rain fromRain Amount.

TurbulenceA range from 0 - 1. This sets how turbulent the transition between weather events is. Low numbers are a smooth, slow transition. Higher numbers are abrupt and fast transitions.

Example:A low number would show a slow gradual change from clear sky to heavy cloud cover. A high number would make the change from clear sky to heavy clouds in a significantly shorter time.

Aurora AmountA range from 0 - 1 to control the amount of aurora. 1 = Strong aurora. 0.1 = very faint aurora.

Aurora ChanceA percentage value (out of 100) of the chance there will be rain in the sky. Rain requiresclouds chanceto be satisfied.

### Water Panel

Selecting Water in the Workspace panel opens up the Water Panel on the right of the screen. See also:Workspace Panel

#### Water Settings

The Water Settings panel allows you to add water sources to your map.

title: Map Creation
section: The Basics of Creating a Map
text:
g Water in the Workspace panel opens up the Water Panel on the right of the screen. See also:Workspace Panel

#### Water Settings

The Water Settings panel allows you to add water sources to your map.

Constant Rate Water SourcesGenerates a constant source of water. Good for the start of rivers or streams that start inside the playable area.

Constant Level Water SourcesGenerates water to a specified height. Used for lakes where you set the height and let it fill up.

Border River Water SourcesGenerally located on the edge of the playable area, this water source generates a flow of water that will flow off the map, or from outside the playable area.

Border Sea Water SourcesGenerally located on the edge of the playable area, this water source generates flow for water that will flow off the map, or from outside the playable area on a much larger scale than Border River Water Sources.

See also:How to use Water Sources

#### Water Simulation Speed

In order to control the Water Simulation, you can now change the water simulation speed separately to time simulation.

Choose from 0x, 1x, 8x, 16x, 32x, 64x, 128x.

### Resources Panel

Selecting Resources in the Workspace panel opens up the Resource Panel on the right of the screen. See also:Workspace Panel

#### Resource Tools

title: Map Creation
section: The Basics of Creating a Map
text:
0x, 1x, 8x, 16x, 32x, 64x, 128x.

### Resources Panel

Selecting Resources in the Workspace panel opens up the Resource Panel on the right of the screen. See also:Workspace Panel

#### Resource Tools

The Resource Tools allow you to paint resources onto your playable area. Similar to Terrain Tool controls you can adjust the brush, brush size and brush strength. Resources may overlap.

- MapCreation Ore Ore Resource Tool - Paint Ore onto your terrain.
- MapCreation Oil Oil Resource Tool - Paint Oil onto your terrain.
- MapCreation FertileLand Fertile Land Resource Tool - Paint Fertile Lane onto your terrain.
- MapCreation Groundwater Groundwater Resource Tool - Paint Groundwater onto your terrain.

#### Resource Import Texture

Instead of manually painting resources on your map terrain, you could use the import button to import a greyscale 16 bit png texture which will apply the selected resource onto the terrain. After you import the texture, you can then still make changes through manually painting with the Resource Tool.

The textures would typically be located in`C://Users/[username]/AppData/LocalLow/Colossal Order/Cities Skylines II/Heightmaps`

On the texture, Black is no resource, white is full resource. When creating a resource texture, best practice would be to use your map's Heightmap as a reference to properly align your resources.

## How to use the Terrain Tools

title: Map Creation
section: How to use the Terrain Tools
text:
resource, white is full resource. When creating a resource texture, best practice would be to use your map's Heightmap as a reference to properly align your resources.

## How to use the Terrain Tools

Selecting the Editor Terrain Modify Terrain tool from theEditor Toolsetwill open the Modify Terrain Panel.

### Terrain Tools

Terrain Tools operate the same in the editor as in the game. See also:Terraforming

### Heightmaps

Terrain heightmaps can be imported and exported.

Heightmaps created with external tools should be placed under Heightmaps/ folder in the game’s appdata directory - typically`C://Users/[username]/AppData/LocalLow/Colossal Order/Cities Skylines II/Heightmaps/`- after which they can be imported from the menu that opens from the Import Heightmap… button.

Height Scale is measured in meters, and is by default 4096. This sets the highest elevation point for your map where 0 is the lowest point and 4096 is the highest.

example:If you set Height Scale to 8000, the lowest point of your map starts at 0m and the highest point would be 8000m. Your heightmap data is stretched to meet the new scale. When creating heightmaps, you can think of black as 0m and white as the highest value on your Height Scale.

Heightmaps exported from the editor will also appear here, containing any modifications made to the terrain with the editor’s terrain tools.

#### Playable area

title: Map Creation
section: How to use the Terrain Tools
text:
he highest value on your Height Scale.

Heightmaps exported from the editor will also appear here, containing any modifications made to the terrain with the editor’s terrain tools.

#### Playable area

Heightmaps should be 4096x4096 resolution, grayscale, 16 bit color channel depth images of .png or .tiff format.

Heightmap only applies to the playable area. Optionally an extended worldmap can also be imported, which contains both the playable area and the surrounding unplayable world.

#### Worldmap

Worldmap should have the same resolution and format as the base heightmap, with the center 1024x1024 area matching the base heightmap.

## How to place Water Sources

In theWater Panelto place a water source you expand the dropdown of the water source type you are wanting to place. Pressing theAdd Item...button will automatically place the water source centered on your current camera position.

To delete all water sources from the specific type, press theclearbutton, or individually remove them with the trashcan icon next to each water source in the list.

Each water source type is colored differently to make it easier to tell them apart.

After placing your water source, you will be able to see the marker show up. Each marker has 3 camera orientated, dynamic controls.

title: Map Creation
text:
e type is colored differently to make it easier to tell them apart.

After placing your water source, you will be able to see the marker show up. Each marker has 3 camera orientated, dynamic controls.

- Mouse over the middle of the marker to see the cross in the middle - allows you to grab and move your water source.
- Mouse over the right or left edge of the marker and use the grab handles to change the diameter of the marker.
- Mouse over the top and bottom edge of the marker and use the grab handles to change the height or rate of the water source.

## Decorating your Map

Decorating maps in Cities Skylines II can be extensive, with access to all objects available in the game using the Add Object button.

### Adding Objects

Pressing Add Object button opens the Add Object Panel.

For ease of usability there are filters, categories and a search bar at the top of the panel. Search is limited by the current filter set, so remember to set category back toAll.

Important Objects for Map Creation

title: Map Creation
text:
ability there are filters, categories and a search bar at the top of the panel. Search is limited by the current filter set, so remember to set category back toAll.

Important Objects for Map Creation

- Ruin decorative buildings found under> Buildings > Misccategory.
- Decorative Props found under> Props > Nature Decorationscategory.
- Trees and Bushes located under> Foliagecategory.'
- Bridges under the> Bridgescategory.
- Roads, road upgrades, intersections and highways are located under> Roadscategory.
- Train, subway and tram tracks are found under> Trackscategory.
- Search "High-Voltage Line" to be able to drawHigh-Voltage Linesand draw them to the edge of the playable area map edge to createElectricity Connections.
- Search "Airplane Outside Connection" to be able to placeAirplane Outside Connectionson the playable area map edge.
- Search "Narrow Seaway", "Medium Seaway" or "Wide Seaway" to be able to draw out seaway networks on your waterways and oceans.

When an object is placed on the map, it will show up in theWorkspace Panelunder a new category> objects.

### Bulldozing Objects

Using the Bulldoze for map creation is simple. When the tool mode shows in the bottom left of your screen, make sure the tool mode is set toMain Element. Typically this is the default tool mode for the bulldozer.

## Saving your Map

title: Map Creation
text:
ion is simple. When the tool mode shows in the bottom left of your screen, make sure the tool mode is set toMain Element. Typically this is the default tool mode for the bulldozer.

## Saving your Map

In theMap Panelafter completing the checklist, adding aPreview image and Thumbnailimage.

Press theSave Mapbutton to open up the Save Map Panel.

Enter a file name. This is separate to the Map Name from the previous Map Panel.

Filename can contain no spaces, only alphanumerical characters, hyphens and underscore.

## Uploading a Map to Paradox Mods

There are two methods for uploading maps toParadox Mods.

### Method 1

1. Load into the editor, and either load a map you have completed or create and finish a new map.
2. Open theMap Panel
3. Press theShare Mapto open the Save Map and Share Panel
4. Enter a file name. This is separate to the Map Name from the previous Map Panel. PressSave and Shareto open the Map Upload Window.
5. Fill out the Name, Short Description and Long Descriptions.
6. Select optional values for Forum links and Links to socials.
7. Optionally add any screenshots to showcase your map in Paradox Mods.
8. PressSubmitand the map will be uploaded to Paradox Mods.

### Method 2

### Method 2

1. Load into the Main Menu, and selectNew Game.
2. Change to theCustom Mapstab.
3. Select the map you wish to share.
4. Press theShare Mapbutton to open the Map Upload Window.
5. Fill out the Name, Short Description and Long Descriptions.
6. Select optional values for Forum links and Links to socials.
7. Optionally add any screenshots to showcase your map in Paradox Mods.
8. PressSubmitand the map will be uploaded to Paradox Mods.

## Uploading an Edit to an Existing Map to Paradox Mods

There are two methods for uploading edits to maps toParadox Mods.

### Method 1

## Uploading an Edit to an Existing Map to Paradox Mods

There are two methods for uploading edits to maps toParadox Mods.

### Method 1

1. Load into the editor, and load into the map you wish to upload an edit of.
2. Open theMap Panel
3. Press theShare Mapto open the Save Map and Share Panel
4. Enter a file name. This is separate to the Map Name from the previous Map Panel. PressSave and Shareto open the Map Upload Window.
5. Fill out the Name and check theUpdate previously shared assetcheckbox.
6. In theExisting asset's IDdropdown, select the asset you are updating.
7. Fill in theVersionnumber. By default your first upload is set to Version 1, so a subsequent update would usually be Version 2.
8. Add some descriptions of the changes you made in theChangelogsection.
9. Fill out the Short Description and Long Descriptions. These can be the same as before or updated.
10. Select optional values for Forum links and Links to socials. These can be the same as before or updated.
11. Optionally add any screenshots to showcase your map in Paradox Mods. These can be the same as before or updated.
12. PressSubmitand the map will be uploaded to Paradox Mods.

title: Maps
text:
# Maps

Source: https://cs2.paradoxwikis.com/Maps

Mapsare the areas where you build your city. Each map is divided into tiles that you can unlock as the game progresses. Each map has 441 unlockable tiles (equivalent to 21×21) covering 159 km².[1] Some game assets and models are locked to thethemeselected for a city, however any map can select any theme with the themes shown below only the default option.

Each zonable cell is 8x8 meters,[2] the same as inCities Skylines.

title: Mediterranean Heritage
text:
# Mediterranean Heritage

Source: https://cs2.paradoxwikis.com/Mediterranean_Heritage

This article istimelessand should be accurate for any version of the game.

Mediterranean Heritage Mediterranean Heritage

DLC banner Mediterranean Heritage Type

Content Creator Pack Release date

2025-03-18Store: Content Creator Pack

Paradox/Steam

TheMediterranean HeritageContent Creator Pack was announced on 2025-03-10[1], alongside theDragon GateandLeisure VenuesCCPs, as well as theAtmospheric Piano Channel,Feelgood Funk RadioandJade Road Radiomusic packs. All were released on 2025-03-18 alongsidePatch 1.2.5f1(aka theAnniversary Patch).

## Official description

Explore the timeless charm of Mediterranean architecture with this Creator Pack, designed by community creator ‘Zarrix’. ‘Mediterranean Heritage’ introduces a range of historic buildings from the 15th to 19th century from regions such as Italy, Spain, Portugal and Southern France. Featuring fluid design and characteristic soft color variations, this pack will bring southern European elegance with a modern flair to your city.

Included in ‘Mediterranean Heritage’ by ‘Zarrix’:

### Medium Housing Zone

title: Mediterranean Heritage
text:
and characteristic soft color variations, this pack will bring southern European elegance with a modern flair to your city.

Included in ‘Mediterranean Heritage’ by ‘Zarrix’:

### Medium Housing Zone

Experience the perfect fusion of tradition and progress as you build a city that honors its heritage while thriving in the present. These buildings are medium residential buildings with five growable levels and feature a soft colour variation that is characteristic for the south of Europe.

### Residential Signature Buildings

Blend historical charm with modern living. These signature buildings have preserved the architectural elements of the past while embracing the future with modern amenities to fit in the heart of city of today.

- Vesuvius Palace
- Seven Hills Residence
- Villa Antiqua
- Imperium Plaza

### Service Buildings

These four service buildings seamlessly blend historical charm with modern functionality and efficiency. With unique upgrades for each structure, you can tailor them to meet the demands of your bustling city while keeping a timeless aesthetic.

- Mediterranean Train Station
- Mediterranean Elementary School
- Mediterranean High School
- Mediterranean Police Station

title: Mod Key Binding
text:
# Mod Key Binding

Source: https://cs2.paradoxwikis.com/Mod_Key_Binding

Verified by Paradox Interactive
This article has been verified by Paradox Interactive for version 1.3.6 f1 of the game.

## Add a key binding

In order to create a keybinding option in your mod settings add a property of ProxyBinding type with getter and setter to your mod settings class

```
public ProxyBinding MyKeyboardBinding { get; set; }

```

It is enough to have a key binding, but it will have default settings and no default key set. The important thing, that no value should be set to this property manually, the game will set it automatically based on attributes.

## Key binding settings

To set different parameters to a key binding use attributes

```
SettingsUIKeyboardBindingAttribute or SettingsUIMouseBinding or SettingsUIGamepadBindingAttribute

[SettingsUIKeyboardBinding(Key. Q, “ActionName”, shift: true)]
public ProxyBinding KeyboardBinding { get; set; }

[SettingsUIMouseBinding(BindingMouse. Forward, “ActionName”)]
public ProxyBinding MouseBinding { get; set; }

[SettingsUIGamepadBinding(GamepadButton. Cross, “ActionName”)]
public ProxyBinding GamepadBinding { get; set; }

```

Next parameters can be set:

title: Mod Key Binding
text:
ame”)]
public ProxyBinding MouseBinding { get; set; }

[SettingsUIGamepadBinding(GamepadButton. Cross, “ActionName”)]
public ProxyBinding GamepadBinding { get; set; }

```

Next parameters can be set:

- Action name - unique string within a mod, all bindings which the same action name will be merged into the same input action
- Default key (keyboard) or button (gamepad)
- Modifiers for keyboard (shift, ctrl, alt) and for gamepad (press left stick)
- Action component for button (press), for axis (negative and positive), for vector2 (up, down, left, right)

## Input action

Few key bindings can be merged into an input action. There are 3 different action types:

- Button - float value between 0 and 1, contains 1 component (press)
- Axis - float value between -1 and 1, contains 2 components (negative and positive)
- Vector2 - vector2 value between (-1;-1) and (1;1), contains 4 components (up, down, left, right)

To merge few key bindings into one input action, all of them should have the same action name set in`SettingsUIKeyboardBindingAttribute or SettingsUIGamepadBindingAttribute`and corresponding action components

```
[SettingsUIKeyboardBinding(Key. DownArrow, AxisComponent. Negative, “AxisAction”, shift: true)]
public ProxyBinding FloatBindingNegative { get; set; }

title: Mod Key Binding
text:
ttribute`and corresponding action components

```
[SettingsUIKeyboardBinding(Key. DownArrow, AxisComponent. Negative, “AxisAction”, shift: true)]
public ProxyBinding FloatBindingNegative { get; set; }

[SettingsUIKeyboardBinding(Key. UpArrow, AxisComponent. Positive, “AxisAction”, shift: true)]
public ProxyBinding FloatBindingPositive { get; set; }

```

## Advanced Input Action Settings

Input actions are not set directly in a settings class, they are generated based on key bindings, but input actions can have additional parameters. To set them add`SettingsUIKeyboardActionAttribute or SettingsUIGamepadActionAttribute`to a setting class (not a key binding property!)

Next parameters can be set:

title: Mod Key Binding
text:
ions can have additional parameters. To set them add`SettingsUIKeyboardActionAttribute or SettingsUIGamepadActionAttribute`to a setting class (not a key binding property!)

Next parameters can be set:

- Action name - it is the main parameter to link action with key bindings, value should be the same as set in`SettingsUIKeyboardBindingAttribute or SettingsUIGamepadBindingAttribute`
- Action type - on of 3 input action types
- Mode - one of 3 modes: DigitalNormalized (value magnitude normalized to have length equal to 1), Digital (0 when key is not pressed and -1/+1 when key is pressed), Analog (all range of values between -1 and 1, it makes sense to use only with gamepad triggers or sticks which support analog values based on pressing force)
- Developer only - use action only in developer mode
- Usages - list of action usages, it is used to resolve binding conflicts.
- Interactions - list of interactions, you can read more about interactions here (Interactions). You can use default interactions or create your own.
- Processors - list of processors, you can read more about interactions here (Processors). You can use default processors or create your own.
- Rebind options - define which part of the binding can be rebound by the user: none, key only, modifiers only, or key & modifiers.
- Can be empty - defines whether the user is allowed to unset this binding.
- Modifier Options - define three possible modifier behaviors:

title: Mod Key Binding
text:
the user: none, key only, modifiers only, or key & modifiers.
- Can be empty - defines whether the user is allowed to unset this binding.
- Modifier Options - define three possible modifier behaviors:

- Disallow - the binding cannot have modifiers (exclusive key usage).
 - Allow - the binding can have modifiers.
 - Ignore - the binding cannot have modifiers (inclusive key usage).

- Note: The difference between the Disallow and Ignore options is that the Disallow option makes a binding conflict with any other binding that uses the same key, regardless of whether the other binding includes modifiers. The Ignore option, on the other hand, does not cause a conflict with other bindings that use the same key combined with modifiers.

## Usages and conflict resolving

The game automatically detects binding conflicts and marks conflict bindings. The game built-in bindings have higher priority to mod bindings and all conflicted mod bindings will be disabled. In case of key binding conflict between mods, conflicts will be marked, but keys will be kept enabled.

Conflict detection is based on usages of key binding. The game has 8 default usages which can be found as constants inGame. Input. Usagesclass, but you are not limited by them, usage can be any unique string or set of strings based on a mod logic.

title: Mod Key Binding
Mod Key Binding
text:
, set theshouldBeEnabledproperty to true. Keep in mind that if an action bindings are conflicting with built-in game bindings it will not be activated.

```
m_ButtonAction.shouldBeEnabled = true;

```

After an action is activated, you can subscribe to the event ProxyAction.onInteraction which will be called when the phase of binding interaction is changed (started, performed, canceled). Or get check action state using one of methods:

- `IsPressed()`
- `WasPressedThisFrame()`
- `WasReleasedThisFrame()`
- `WasPerformedThisFrame()`

The value of action can be obtained using the ReadValue<T>() method. Take in consideration that right type parameter should be passed for each type of action: float for Button and Axis, vector2 for Vector

```
m_ButtonAction. ReadValue<float>()
m_AxisAction. ReadValue<float>()
m_VectorAction. ReadValue<Vector2>()

```

## Localization

There is few new helpers methods to get localization ID for actions

- `GetBindingMapLocaleID`- get ID a map of all actions of a mod
- `GetBindingKeyLocaleID`- get ID for an action

## Mimic Keybinding

title: Mod Key Binding
text:
here is few new helpers methods to get localization ID for actions

- `GetBindingMapLocaleID`- get ID a map of all actions of a mod
- `GetBindingKeyLocaleID`- get ID for an action

## Mimic Keybinding

Mods can not use the game's built-in actions for their purposes, but sometimes it is necessary to keep a binding value the same as a built-in action. In this case binding can mimic another binding. Every time when a user sets a value to the main binding, a mimic binding will get the same value. Use SettingsUIBindingMimicAttribute to set which binding it should be synced with. It is recommended to make such an option hidden or disabled so that a user could not set it directly. Take into account that the default value for such an option will be ignored.

```
 [SettingsUIMouseBinding(TestSettingsMod.kApplyMimicAction)]
 [SettingsUIBindingMimic(InputManager.kNavigationMap, "Mouse Primary Click")]
 [SettingsUIDisableByCondition(typeof(Setting), nameof(DisabledMimic))]
 public ProxyBinding ApplyMimic { get; set; }

```

## Example

Mod security
text:
# Mod security

Source: https://cs2.paradoxwikis.com/Mod_security

## Trusted Sources

The safest way to obtain mods is through the official channels that the community already trusts.

- Paradox Modshttps://mods.paradoxplaza.com/(also known as pdx mods) is the primary hub for CS2 mods. It is actively moderated and each mod is verified before it appears on the site. Most mods have a page on Paradox's official forumhttps://forum.paradoxplaza.com/or to discord where you can check user reviews and directly communicate with the author.

Mods are free to download. Do not use external download platforms. Do not use sites that request personal information or payment.

- skyveis a companion tool that works hand‑in‑hand with Paradox Mods. It scans your library for conflicts, outdated files, and missing dependencies.
- Discord can be used when the mod author is well‑known, the source code is publicly available on GitHub, and the Discord channel is the author’s official release channel (often used for pre‑release testing).

Avoid any other sites that promise the same mods but lack a clear moderation process. Only use links from YouTube or Reddit when they point to Paradox Mods. Do not use file-sharing platforms (e.g. X-Drive, X-Sharing, etc.). Don't hesitate to use Paradox's forums or Discord servers if you want to read reviews from other users and ensure that the mods are reliable.

title: Mod security
text:
se file-sharing platforms (e.g. X-Drive, X-Sharing, etc.). Don't hesitate to use Paradox's forums or Discord servers if you want to read reviews from other users and ensure that the mods are reliable.

## Avoid Out‑of‑Date Mods

BepInEx & BepInEx mods – do not use

Verify that your mod is the current version: there may be older, non‑recommended versions: on the mod’s page (Paradox Mods), the information about the recommended game version appears at the top of the description; older versions may be present.

If you are using a beta or pre‑release version, keep in mind that it may contain undetected errors and might not be compatible with your current installation.

Finally, consult the update notes or the mod discussion to see if there are known issues.

Mods that add assets for the Extra Assets Importer or that provide new maps may be marked “outdated” simply because the version number in their description hasn’t been updated. Skyve will flag these as outdated, but they are harmless and they normally work fine. In short, use Skyve to distinguish between genuinely obsolete mods and those that are only cosmetically “outdated.”

## Skyve – Recommended Tool

skyvecomes with the same PDX Mods capabilities as the game. Which means you'll be able to browse PDX Mods directly from Skyve. It has advanced and automatic backup features. It has advanced compatibility management:

title: Mod security
text:
the same PDX Mods capabilities as the game. Which means you'll be able to browse PDX Mods directly from Skyve. It has advanced and automatic backup features. It has advanced compatibility management:

- Warning you that a mod is not compatible with the latest patch of the game.
- Letting you know if a mod is broken or deprecated.
- Warning you if two or more mods that you are using are incompatible with each other, or if they have the same functionalities.
- Cautioning you about mods that break your save if you stop using them.
- Telling you that a mod you're using is succeeded by another newer mod.
- Letting you know if you're missing any requirements for your active mods.

Lets you create separate mod playsets (e.g., “Playtest”, “Stable”).

Stick to Paradox Mods, Skyve, and verified Discord channels. Do not use BepInEx. Use Skyve to spot known bug, real vs. cosmetic “outdated” tags, and be wary of mods with known instability.

title: Modding
text:
# Modding

Source: https://cs2.paradoxwikis.com/Modding

AdvisorNotifications

Warning: Beta Documentation

This page is showing beta documentation for creating Code Mods, Maps, Assets and Prefabs. Some information may be subject to change. Remember to carefully back up any files you test with.

## Create what you imagine

Cities: Skylines II gives you the ability to create, use and share custom content.
With the all-inclusive in-game Editor you will be able to create spectacular maps and assets.

- The all-in-one Editor will allow you to make maps and a variety of assets
- TheModding Toolchainwill allow you to create Code Mods that modify gameplay
- Paradox Modswill help you download, use and share custom content

These modding pages will be considered a 'live document' that will be updated regularly and added to as new content is developed.

## Official Resources

Official Resources have been reviewed by Paradox Interactive. Each page has a badge stating which game version the information was verified against. These pages will update alongside the continued development of the game.

### Code Mods
